what_it_does: A glycol that acts as a humectant and solvent while also helping to preserve formulas, popular in K-beauty as a gentler alternative to traditional preservatives.

A stable, oil- and water-soluble vitamin C derivative that retains more of ascorbic acid's potency than many other esters while being gentler and more shelf-stable; popular in modern brightening serums.

A potent tyrosinase-inhibiting brightening ingredient, often considered one of the stronger non-hydroquinone options for fading dark spots and melasma; generally well tolerated but can be irritating for very sensitive skin.
pregnancy_notes: Limited dedicated pregnancy safety research; caution advised due to insufficient data despite generally favorable topical tolerance profile.

what_it_does: A relatively novel acid used in scalp and skin formulas to support a balanced microbiome and gently support cell turnover; used at low percentages.
pregnancy_notes: Limited safety data; general caution advised for novel actives during pregnancy.

what_it_does: An older chemical UVB filter once widely used in EU sunscreens; falling out of favor due to hormone-activity concerns identified in newer research.
pregnancy_notes: Shown in animal studies to have estrogenic and thyroid-disrupting activity; the EU has reclassified this filter as prohibited (moved from the allowed list to the banned-substances annex), with phase-out dates through 2026.

what_it_does: A 'Botox-like' neurotransmission-inhibiting peptide that aims to relax expression lines such as crow's feet over weeks of use, though clinical evidence for significant wrinkle reduction is modest compared to injectable neuromodulators.
pregnancy_notes: No robust pregnancy-specific safety data exists for this peptide; many formulators and prenatal-safe skincare guides suggest avoiding novel neuropeptides during pregnancy out of caution.

what_it_does: A peptide marketed with a similar mechanism to Acetyl Hexapeptide-8, aiming to soften the look of expression lines by affecting neurotransmitter release at the skin surface.
pregnancy_notes: No dedicated pregnancy safety data; general caution applied to novel neuropeptides during pregnancy.

what_it_does: A peptide marketed to support skin around the eye area, with proposed effects on supporting the look of firmness and reducing puffiness signs.
pregnancy_notes: No dedicated pregnancy safety data; general caution applied to novel peptides.

what_it_does: A peptide marketed primarily for the under-eye area to reduce the appearance of puffiness by supporting microcirculation and lymphatic-related skin signaling.
pregnancy_notes: No dedicated pregnancy safety data; general caution applied to novel peptides.

what_it_does: A film-forming synthetic polymer used to thicken formulas and improve the longevity of sunscreens and makeup by increasing water resistance.
A versatile carbomer-type thickener that gels water-based and emulsion formulas while also helping stabilize oil droplets, giving a smooth, cushiony texture.
Baobab seed oil — a moderate-weight, antioxidant-containing oil traditionally used to soften and condition dry skin and hair.
A synthetic third-generation retinoid used to treat acne by normalizing cell turnover and reducing clogged pores; available over-the-counter at 0.1% in some markets.
pregnancy_notes: Classified as a retinoid with teratogenic potential in animal studies; prescribing guidance advises discontinuing use during pregnancy.

what_it_does: A well-tolerated anti-aging active that helps smooth fine lines and supports skin energy and recovery. Effective at very low concentrations.
An organic acid sometimes used as a pH adjuster or buffering agent in cosmetic formulas. Low irritation potential and primarily a functional, behind-the-scenes ingredient.
Denatured ethanol — a drying solvent that gives a quick-absorbing, weightless feel but can compromise the skin barrier with frequent use, especially on dry or sensitive skin.
A broad category of marine algae extracts used for hydration, antioxidant protection, and mineral-rich skin conditioning.
A soothing, skin-protecting ingredient that calms irritation and supports healing. Very gentle and good for sensitive skin.
Soothing, hydrating aloe vera that calms irritation and provides lightweight moisture. Pleasant for inflamed or sun-exposed skin.
A close relative of aloe vera, this leaf extract offers similar soothing and hydrating benefits for irritated or sun-exposed skin.

A synthetically stabilized, more potent form of arbutin (a derivative of hydroquinone) that inhibits tyrosinase to reduce hyperpigmentation and even skin tone; well tolerated and considered safer and more stable than natural beta-arbutin.
pregnancy_notes: Structurally related to hydroquinone; while topical arbutin releases only trace amounts of hydroquinone, many dermatologists advise caution and suggest discussing with a doctor during pregnancy.

what_it_does: A synthetic fragrance ingredient with a soft violet, woody scent used widely in perfumed skincare and cosmetic formulas.
pregnancy_notes: No specific pregnancy contraindication, but best minimized in sensitized skin.

what_it_does: A pH adjuster and neutralizer commonly used to thicken and stabilize gel formulas containing carbomer. Generally low-irritation at the small amounts used.
A modern synthetic thickener that gels formulas, including those high in alcohol or electrolytes, giving a light, cushiony, non-tacky texture often used in gel moisturizers.
A strong alkaline pH adjuster used in small, controlled amounts to neutralize acidic ingredients. Can be irritating to skin and eyes, particularly if a formula is poorly buffered.
A buffered salt form of lactic acid used in prescription and OTC formulas for keratosis pilaris and dry, rough skin, providing gentler exfoliation than free lactic acid at the same concentration.
pregnancy_notes: As a lactic acid derivative, generally considered acceptable at typical leave-on concentrations, but checking with a provider is advised for prescription-strength formulas.

what_it_does: A milder ethoxylated cousin of ammonium lauryl sulfate that cleanses and foams well while being somewhat gentler on skin and hair than non-ethoxylated sulfates.
A strong foaming and cleansing surfactant from the sulfate family, effective at removing oil and dirt but can be drying and irritating with frequent use, especially on sensitive skin.
An amino-functionalized silicone mainly used in hair care to smooth and detangle, but also appears in some skincare for its conditioning, friction-reducing slip.
A synthetic fragrance ingredient with a jasmine-like floral odor, used to add a soft floral note to perfumed cosmetics.
pregnancy_notes: No specific pregnancy contraindication, but a recognized contact allergen.

what_it_does: A fragrance ingredient with a floral scent, the alcohol counterpart of amyl cinnamal, used in perfumed cosmetic products.
pregnancy_notes: No specific pregnancy contraindication, but best minimized in sensitized skin.

what_it_does: Pineapple fruit extract that provides natural bromelain enzyme for gentle protein-based exfoliation, plus mild brightening antioxidants.
Dong quai root extract used in skincare for mild antioxidant and skin-conditioning benefits, though it can increase photosensitivity in concentrated forms.
pregnancy_notes: Often advised against in pregnancy due to traditional use as a uterine stimulant; caution applies even topically.

what_it_does: A fragrance ingredient with a sweet, floral anise-like scent, used as a fragrance component in perfumed cosmetics.
pregnancy_notes: No specific pregnancy contraindication, but best minimized in sensitized skin.

what_it_does: Roman chamomile flower extract used for its gentle soothing and calming effect on irritated or reactive skin.
A naturally derived emulsifier and stabilizer often used alongside behenyl alcohol to thicken and stabilize lotions and creams with a 'clean' formulation profile.

The naturally occurring form of arbutin (found in bearberry and other plants) that inhibits tyrosinase to lighten dark spots; generally less stable and slightly less potent than the synthetic alpha form.
pregnancy_notes: Related to hydroquinone metabolism; caution advised during pregnancy as with alpha-arbutin, though topical use poses low systemic risk.

what_it_does: Argan oil — a lightweight, vitamin E-rich oil from Moroccan argan tree kernels that nourishes and softens skin and hair without feeling greasy.
An amino acid used as a gentle pH adjuster (an alternative to triethanolamine in some 'clean' formulas) and skin-conditioning agent. Very well tolerated.
A mugwort species extract used similarly for its calming and antioxidant benefits in soothing serums and sheet masks.
Mugwort leaf extract, a staple of Korean skincare prized for calming sensitive and reactive skin and supporting a healthier barrier.
Pure vitamin C — a potent antioxidant that brightens, evens tone, and boosts collagen. Effective but unstable and can sting sensitive skin at low pH.

A vitamin C derivative formed by binding ascorbic acid to glucose, making it stable in formulas; enzymes in skin slowly release active vitamin C for antioxidant and brightening benefits with low irritation potential.
A silicon-bound vitamin C derivative marketed for improved stability and slow-release antioxidant and firming benefits; generally well tolerated.
A fat-soluble ester of vitamin C and palmitic acid used mainly as an antioxidant to stabilize oils in formulas; weaker brightening evidence than water-soluble vitamin C forms but very gentle.
An oil-soluble, highly stable vitamin C derivative often used in anhydrous and oil-based formulas for antioxidant and brightening support; generally well tolerated.
Rooibos tea extract with antioxidant polyphenols used to help protect skin from oxidative stress while offering mild soothing benefits.
An alternative listing for rooibos leaf extract, used the same way for antioxidant and soothing skin benefits.
A potent carotenoid antioxidant derived from algae that helps neutralize free radicals and may support collagen and skin elasticity; gentle with a low irritation profile.
A botanical extract used in antioxidant and anti-aging formulas for its polysaccharide and flavonoid content, believed to support skin resilience; generally gentle.
Colloidal oat extract that forms a protective film and is well documented for relieving itch, redness, and irritation, especially in sensitive or eczema-prone skin.
Finely ground oat flour used in cleansers and masks for gentle soothing and mild physical exfoliation.

A chemical UVA filter that absorbs the full UVA spectrum, protecting against deep skin aging and DNA damage from sun exposure. It is photo-unstable on its own and is usually paired with stabilizers like octocrylene.
A multitasking active that fights acne, calms rosacea-related redness, and fades post-acne marks. Gentle enough for sensitive skin.
A water-soluble derivative of azelaic acid designed to offer similar anti-redness and brightening benefits with better formulation stability and lower irritation than free azelaic acid.
A gentle, babassu-oil-derived amphoteric surfactant used as a sulfate alternative to add mild cleansing and a soft, conditioning lather.
A plant-derived retinol alternative that offers similar smoothing and anti-aging benefits with far less irritation, and is considered suitable when retinoids aren't.
Bamboo extract rich in silica, used for its mild soothing, antioxidant, and mattifying skin-conditioning properties.
Bamboo sap water used as a light, mineral-rich hydrating base liquid in toners and essences.
A cationic conditioning agent and emulsifier mainly used in hair care, occasionally found in skincare emulsions for its conditioning, smoothing properties.
An absorbent volcanic clay that swells in water and binds oil and impurities, widely used in clay masks for deep-cleansing and mattifying effects.

A quaternary ammonium preservative and surfactant with strong antimicrobial action, common in eye and contact-lens products as well as some leave-on skincare. Can be irritating to skin and eyes with repeated use, especially at higher concentrations.
pregnancy_notes: Generally considered low systemic risk, but its irritation potential with frequent or prolonged use is the main reason for caution, regardless of pregnancy status.

what_it_does: An isothiazolinone-class preservative used mainly in rinse-off products and industrial formulas, with a known potential to cause allergic contact dermatitis in sensitized individuals.
pregnancy_notes: Sensitization risk applies to all users; no specific pregnancy data but caution is reasonable given the allergen profile.

what_it_does: A naturally occurring acid (also produced synthetically) used as a preservative to inhibit mold and bacteria, most effective at acidic pH. Widely used in foods and cosmetics with a strong safety record.

A broad-spectrum chemical UV filter that also protects formulas from UV-induced degradation. It has the most-studied hormone-disruption and sensitization profile of any UV filter and is being phased out of many newer sunscreen formulas.
pregnancy_notes: Detected in blood, urine, amniotic fluid, and breast milk after use; studies link maternal exposure to altered birth weight and possible endocrine effects. Many obstetric and dermatology sources suggest choosing alternative filters during pregnancy.

what_it_does: A potent antibacterial and keratolytic acne treatment that kills C. acnes bacteria and helps unclog pores; can be very drying and bleaches fabric and hair.
pregnancy_notes: Systemic absorption is minimal, and many dermatologists consider low concentrations (under 5%) reasonably safe, but some clinicians still advise checking with a provider, especially in the first trimester.

what_it_does: A preservative and solvent with a faint floral scent. Generally well tolerated, but counts among the EU declarable fragrance allergens.
A fragrance ingredient and solvent with a faint balsamic scent, used to help dissolve and stabilize other fragrance compounds.
pregnancy_notes: No specific pregnancy contraindication, but best minimized in sensitized skin.

what_it_does: A fragrance ingredient with a sweet balsamic, cinnamon-like scent naturally found in balsam of Peru, used as a fixative in perfumes.
pregnancy_notes: No specific pregnancy contraindication, but best minimized in sensitized skin.

what_it_does: A fragrance ingredient with a mild floral balsamic scent, often used as a fixative to help other fragrance notes last longer on the skin.
pregnancy_notes: No specific pregnancy contraindication, but best minimized in sensitized skin.

what_it_does: A water-soluble UVB filter chemically related to camphor derivatives, used in European sun care to enhance broad-spectrum SPF performance.
pregnancy_notes: Limited specific pregnancy data exists for this filter; as with other camphor-derivative UV filters, moderate use within regulated limits is generally considered low risk, but data gaps warrant caution.

what_it_does: Beet root extract used as a natural source of betaine, a hydrating and skin-conditioning compound, sometimes also valued for mild natural color.
A polysaccharide derived from oats or yeast that hydrates, soothes irritation, and supports the skin barrier. Good for sensitive and reactive skin.
A naturally derived humectant (from sugar beets) that hydrates and softens skin while also helping to buffer the irritation potential of surfactants in cleansers.
A gentler, less irritating derivative of salicylic acid that provides similar pore-clearing and anti-inflammatory benefits with a milder feel, popular in sensitive-skin BHA formulas.
pregnancy_notes: As a salicylic-acid derivative, generally given the same precautionary guidance as salicylic acid — low leave-on use is usually considered acceptable, but checking with a provider is advised.

what_it_does: A fermented probiotic-derived ingredient (popularized in essence formulas) known for hydrating, antioxidant, and skin-barrier-supporting benefits; generally gentle and well tolerated.
Vitamin B7, included in skincare mainly as a conditioning agent; well known for haircare/nail benefits when ingested, but topical evidence for skin benefits is limited. Very gentle.

A broad-spectrum, highly photostable chemical UV filter covering both UVA and UVB; well regarded for low irritation and excellent stability in formulas. Not approved for use in the US as of this writing.
A silicone emulsifier that helps disperse mineral sunscreen filters or oils evenly, giving a smooth, non-greasy finish in sunscreens and primers.
A naturally occurring compound from chamomile with soothing, anti-inflammatory, and mild antioxidant properties; very gentle and often added to calm irritation from actives.
Borage seed oil — one of the richest natural sources of gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), used in small amounts to support barrier function and soothe dry, sensitive skin.
Frankincense (boswellia) resin extract used for its anti-inflammatory boswellic acids, valued in calming, redness-reducing formulas.
pregnancy_notes: Limited topical safety data in pregnancy; traditional internal use is linked to uterine effects, so caution is reasonable.

what_it_does: A proteolytic enzyme derived from pineapple stems that breaks down dead skin cell proteins for gentle chemical-free exfoliation; can cause stinging or irritation on broken skin.

A fast-acting preservative effective against bacteria, sometimes used in combination with other preservatives. Can release small amounts of formaldehyde under certain formulation conditions and carries some sensitization risk.
pregnancy_notes: Possible trace formaldehyde release under certain conditions; many people choose more established alternatives during pregnancy out of caution.

what_it_does: A lightweight humectant and solvent that boosts hydration and helps other ingredients penetrate. Gives a non-sticky finish.

A longer-chain paraben preservative with the strongest estrogenic activity of the common parabens, which is why EU regulation specifically restricts its concentration and bans it from leave-on nappy-area products for young children.
pregnancy_notes: Has the most measurable hormone-mimicking activity among common parabens; many people choose to avoid it during pregnancy as a precaution even though risk at cosmetic-use levels is considered low.

what_it_does: A synthetic musky, floral fragrance ingredient once very common in perfumes and cosmetics; restricted in the EU due to reproductive toxicity and sensitization concerns.
pregnancy_notes: Classified as a reproductive toxicant (CMR) by the EU; banned from EU cosmetics since March 2022. Avoid during pregnancy.

what_it_does: The official INCI form of shea butter, used as a rich emollient that softens skin and supports barrier repair. Moderately comedogenic for some.
A lightweight, non-greasy ester emollient that helps dissolve sunscreen filters and gives products a silky, dry-touch finish.
A silicone elastomer thickener used to gel oils and add a cushiony, soft-focus texture, popular in modern sunscreen and primer formulas.
An antioxidant that temporarily constricts blood vessels, often used in eye products to reduce the look of puffiness and dark circles.
A buffered, non-acidic vitamin C salt that provides gentle antioxidant benefits with less irritation potential than pure ascorbic acid, though generally considered less potent for brightening.
A mineral powder used as a gentle abrasive in scrubs and toothpaste and as a bulking/opacifying agent in powders.

A calcium-bound form of EDTA used as a chelating agent, functioning the same way as other EDTA salts to bind stray metal ions and stabilize a formula. Considered safe and gentle at cosmetic use levels.
A salt form of pantothenic acid (vitamin B5) used for its skin-conditioning and soothing properties, similar in function to panthenol but less common in modern formulas.
A calcium salt of sorbic acid used as an alternative preservative against mold and yeast, functioning similarly to potassium sorbate. Less common but considered comparably gentle.
Marigold flower extract long used in herbal medicine to soothe irritated skin, support healing, and calm minor inflammation.
Camellia seed oil, a lightweight, fast-absorbing emollient traditionally used in Japanese skincare to soften and protect skin without heaviness.
Tea seed oil used as an emollient that contains some antioxidant polyphenols; primarily a moisturizing carrier oil rather than a potent active.
Green tea extract, rich in polyphenols (catechins like EGCG) that provide antioxidant and soothing benefits, helping calm inflammation and protect against environmental damage.
A hydrosol distilled from green tea leaves used as a mild antioxidant and soothing base liquid in formulas; gentler and less concentrated than the leaf extract.
Oil pressed from green tea seeds, used as an emollient with some antioxidant content; richer and more occlusive than the leaf extract or water forms, so may be less ideal for acne-prone skin.
A cooling, pungent compound derived from the camphor tree, used for its tingling sensation and mild antimicrobial properties in balms and treatments.
pregnancy_notes: Large topical amounts are best avoided in pregnancy; can be irritating to sensitive skin.

what_it_does: Hemp seed oil — a balanced, lightweight oil rich in omega-3 and omega-6 fatty acids that moisturizes without feeling heavy. Contains no THC.
A coconut-derived chelating and preservative-boosting ingredient often marketed as a 'natural' alternative to EDTA, helping other preservatives work more effectively. Gentle and well tolerated.
A medium-chain fatty acid with mild antimicrobial properties, sometimes used in acne-targeted formulas or as an emollient component derived from coconut.
A lightweight, silky emollient derived from coconut and glycerin that softens skin and disperses other ingredients. Well tolerated and low comedogenicity.
A lightweight, plant-derived emollient ester blend that gives a smooth, fast-absorbing finish similar to caprylic/capric triglyceride.
A modern biodegradable, silicone-like polymer emollient that gives a smooth, soft-focus finish without using actual silicone.
An amino-acid-derived ingredient used for its mild antimicrobial and skin-conditioning properties, often included in acne-targeted formulas as a gentler alternative to harsher actives.
A lightweight ester emollient blend that absorbs quickly and gives a smooth, dry-touch finish in serums and lotions.
A multifunctional ingredient that moisturizes skin and boosts the effectiveness of preservatives, frequently paired with phenoxyethanol or used in preservative-free formulas.
A lightweight, fast-spreading silicone that gives a dry, silky finish without the static-prone feel some other silicones can leave, often used as a lighter dimethicone alternative.
A mild sugar-derived surfactant used for gentle cleansing and as a co-emulsifier, often appearing in natural and sensitive-skin formulations.
A synthetic thickener used to create smooth gels and stabilize emulsions. Inert and non-irritating.
Papaya fruit extract that supplies natural papain enzyme along with antioxidants, used in enzyme exfoliating masks and cleansers.
A naturally occurring dipeptide with antioxidant and anti-glycation properties, helping protect collagen from sugar-related cross-linking damage that contributes to skin aging.
A plant-derived powder or fiber used as a gentle exfoliant, oil absorber, or thickener in scrubs, powders, and gel formulas.
Extract of cica/gotu kola, rich in soothing compounds that calm redness, support healing, and strengthen the barrier. A K-beauty favorite for sensitive skin.
Beeswax — a natural occlusive wax that thickens balms and locks in moisture. Moderately comedogenic, so it can be problematic for very acne-prone skin.
A skin-identical lipid that rebuilds and reinforces the moisture barrier, reducing dryness and sensitivity. A key barrier-repair ingredient.
A widely used nonionic emulsifier that helps stabilize oil-in-water emulsions and improve the consistency of creams and lotions.
A fatty alcohol used to thicken and stabilize creams and as an emollient. Despite the name it is non-drying and soothing — not the same as drying alcohols.
A silicone elastomer powder that absorbs oil and blurs the look of pores and fine lines, giving a soft-focus, matte velvety finish to primers and sunscreens.
A lightweight ester emollient that smooths and softens skin while improving the spreadability of creams without a greasy after-feel.
A sugar-derived, plant-based emulsifier used to create stable, naturally derived oil-in-water emulsions with a light, non-greasy finish.
A cationic surfactant primarily used as a conditioning agent in hair products, with occasional use in skincare as a mild antistatic emulsifier.
A silicone wax that helps emulsify water-in-silicone formulas and gives a rich, cushiony slip, commonly used in sunscreens and primers.
A lightweight, non-greasy ester emollient that mimics the feel of natural skin lipids and improves the spreadability of creams and lotions.
A modified cellulose gum used as a thickener and mild emulsion stabilizer, often found in lotions to give a richer, more cushiony texture.
A wax-like emollient ester that thickens formulas and gives a smooth, slightly cushioning texture. Mildly comedogenic for some users.
A silicone-based polymeric emulsifier that helps stabilize water-in-silicone formulas, common in modern lightweight sunscreens and primers.
German chamomile flower extract containing bisabolol and chamazulene, known for calming redness, soothing irritation, and mild antioxidant activity.
A finely milled carbon powder, often activated, used in cleansers and masks for its ability to physically adsorb oil, dirt, and impurities from the skin's surface.
A nutrient-dense algae extract providing antioxidant and skin-conditioning benefits; generally well tolerated and used to support overall skin resilience.

A strong antiseptic and preservative used mainly in medicated or antibacterial formulas. Effective but can cause irritation or, rarely, allergic reactions with frequent use, and isn't typically found in everyday leave-on skincare.
pregnancy_notes: Mainly a concern for irritation and rare hypersensitivity reactions; used cautiously and only when medically indicated.

what_it_does: A preservative effective against bacteria and fungi, sometimes used as a paraben alternative. Generally well tolerated, though it has a slightly higher irritation potential than phenoxyethanol at higher concentrations.
Chrysanthemum flower extract used for its antioxidant and calming properties, popular in East Asian skin-soothing toners and essences.
A red synthetic pigment used in lipsticks and blushes; insoluble in water, making it a stable, long-lasting colorant choice.
A red synthetic dye, typically used in its calcium lake form, common in lipsticks, blushes, and nail polishes for a true-red shade.
An orange-red synthetic dye used to add warm coral and orange tones to lipsticks, blushes, and other color cosmetics.
A bright red-orange synthetic dye widely used to color lipsticks, blushes, eyeshadows, and self-tanners.
pregnancy_notes: Approved for cosmetic use, but as a synthetic azo dye it can trigger contact sensitivity in some users; generally considered low-risk topically in pregnancy, but those who prefer to limit synthetic dyes may choose mineral-pigment alternatives.

what_it_does: A bright yellow synthetic dye used to color cosmetics, often as an aluminum lake form; one of the most common synthetic colorants in makeup and personal care products.
pregnancy_notes: Approved for cosmetic use by the FDA and EU, but as a synthetic azo-related dye it has a history of triggering allergic skin reactions in sensitive individuals; no specific pregnancy contraindication exists topically, but those with dye sensitivities may want to avoid it.

what_it_does: A bright blue synthetic dye used in small amounts to create blue and to balance other shades in eyeshadows, soaps, and color cosmetics.
pregnancy_notes: Approved for cosmetic use, but can be a sensitizer in dye-sensitive individuals; no specific pregnancy restriction exists for topical use, though minimizing synthetic dye exposure is a common precaution some choose to take.

what_it_does: A bright pink-red synthetic dye, often called eosin, traditionally used in long-wear and stain-type lip products for its skin-staining property.
pregnancy_notes: Generally permitted, but eosin dyes have a documented potential for photosensitivity (lip staining and irritation when combined with sun exposure) in a subset of users.

what_it_does: A pink-red synthetic dye commonly used in its lake form to color lipsticks, blushes, and gloss products with a cool-toned pink.
A deep blue synthetic pigment derived from indigo dye chemistry, used to create cool blue tones in eyeshadows and other color cosmetics.
A red synthetic pigment derived from indigo chemistry, used to create deep red shades in lipsticks and other color cosmetics.
Carmine, a natural red pigment derived from the cochineal insect, used in lipsticks, blushes, and other color cosmetics to create rich red shades.
pregnancy_notes: Naturally derived and generally well tolerated, but carmine is a recognized cause of allergic and anaphylactic-type reactions in a small subset of sensitive individuals.

what_it_does: Chlorophyllin-copper complex, a green pigment derived from plant chlorophyll, occasionally used to add a natural-looking green tint to cosmetics.
Aluminum powder, a metallic mineral pigment used in small amounts to create bright, reflective shimmer or frosted effects in makeup.
Ultramarine blue, a vivid mineral pigment derived from sodium aluminum silicate, used in eyeshadows, mascaras, and colored powders.
Bismuth oxychloride, a pearlescent white-silver pigment that gives foundations and highlighters a soft, shimmering glow.
pregnancy_notes: Generally considered safe, though it is a known mild irritant for some users with sensitive or rosacea-prone skin due to its sharp, plate-like particle shape.

what_it_does: Carbon black, a deep-black pigment derived from carbon used in mascaras, eyeliners, and other dark color cosmetics.
Chromium oxide green, a mineral pigment used to create green shades in eyeshadows and other color cosmetics.
Chromium hydroxide green, a mineral pigment that produces a cooler, more bluish-green shade than chromium oxide green; used in eye and face makeup.
Bronze powder, a metallic mineral pigment used in small amounts to create warm, bronzed shimmer effects in highlighters and bronzers.
Red iron oxide, a natural mineral pigment used to add warm red-brown tones to foundations, blushes, concealers, and tinted moisturizers.
Yellow iron oxide, a natural mineral pigment used to give makeup and tinted skincare a warm, golden-yellow undertone.
Black iron oxide, a natural mineral pigment used to darken and add depth to foundations, mascaras, eyeliners, and brow products.
Ferric ferrocyanide, known as Prussian blue, a deep-blue mineral pigment occasionally used in eye and nail color cosmetics.
Manganese violet, a mineral pigment used to create purple shades in eyeshadows, blushes, and other color cosmetics.
Silver powder, a metallic pigment used sparingly in cosmetics to create cool-toned shimmer and metallic finishes in eye and face products.

A bright white mineral pigment used to lighten, opacify, and add coverage to makeup, powders, and tinted sunscreens; chemically the same compound as the sunscreen active titanium dioxide but used here purely as a colorant.
Also known as cinnamaldehyde, this fragrance compound gives a warm cinnamon scent and is one of the more potent fragrance sensitizers used in cosmetics.
pregnancy_notes: No specific pregnancy contraindication, but a strong known sensitizer; best avoided by reactive skin.

what_it_does: Cinnamon leaf essential oil used for fragrance, but it is one of the more potent skin sensitizers among essential oils and frequently causes contact irritation.
pregnancy_notes: Essential oils with high sensitization potential are best avoided in skincare during pregnancy.

what_it_does: A fragrance ingredient with a soft floral, hyacinth-like scent naturally found in cinnamon and balsam, used to perfume cosmetic products.
pregnancy_notes: No specific pregnancy contraindication, but a recognized contact allergen.

what_it_does: A citrus-scented fragrance compound found naturally in lemongrass and citrus oils, commonly used to add a fresh top note to fragrance.
pregnancy_notes: No specific pregnancy contraindication, but a recognized contact allergen, especially when oxidized.

what_it_does: Mainly used in tiny amounts to adjust a product's pH. At higher levels it acts as a mild AHA exfoliant and antioxidant.
A fragrance ingredient with a mild, sweet floral scent derived from rose and geranium oils, used to perfume cosmetics and personal care products.
pregnancy_notes: No specific pregnancy contraindication, but best minimized in sensitized skin.

what_it_does: Watermelon fruit extract used for its hydrating, antioxidant, and lightly cooling effect on skin.
Bergamot essential oil prized for fragrance, but it is highly phototoxic and a known fragrance allergen that can cause photosensitivity reactions on sun-exposed skin.
pregnancy_notes: Essential oils, especially phototoxic citrus oils, are best used with caution and avoided on sun-exposed skin.

what_it_does: Orange fruit extract used as a mild natural source of AHAs and antioxidants in gentle exfoliating formulas.
Sweet orange peel extract containing vitamin C and flavonoids, used for antioxidant and mild brightening benefits; can occasionally be sensitizing due to citrus-derived compounds.

Lemon peel extract containing vitamin C and citrus compounds used for antioxidant and brightening support; can be sensitizing or phototoxic in some formulations, so leave-on use at higher concentrations warrants caution.
Lemon peel essential oil used for fragrance and brightening claims, but it is phototoxic and a known sensitizer, especially in leave-on, sun-exposed products.
pregnancy_notes: Phototoxic citrus oils are best used cautiously and avoided before sun exposure.

what_it_does: Satsuma mandarin peel extract used for its vitamin C and flavonoid content, providing antioxidant and brightening support; generally gentle.
An antifungal preservative-adjacent ingredient used mainly in anti-dandruff hair products to control the yeast associated with flaking and itching. Not typically used in facial skincare.
pregnancy_notes: Some regulatory bodies have flagged climbazole for endocrine-disruption screening; limiting use during pregnancy is a reasonable precaution.

what_it_does: A foam-boosting and thickening agent derived from coconut, used in cleansers to increase lather density; some formulations have raised concerns over trace nitrosamine contamination.
pregnancy_notes: DEA compounds can form trace nitrosamines under certain conditions; many brands have phased it out as a precaution.

what_it_does: A coconut-derived foam booster and viscosity enhancer used in shampoos and cleansers to create denser, more luxurious lather.
A mild, coconut-derived secondary surfactant that boosts foam and softens harsher cleansers. Can occasionally sensitize. Common in gentle face washes.
A gentle, coconut-derived secondary surfactant from the sultaine family that boosts foam and reduces irritation from stronger detergents in a formula.
A mild, coconut- and sugar-derived cleansing agent that produces gentle foam, well suited to sensitive skin and used widely in modern low-irritation cleansers.
A coconut-derived amphoteric surfactant similar to cocamidopropyl betaine, used to gently cleanse and stabilize foam without excess irritation.
A lightweight, coconut-derived ester emollient with a dry, silky finish; despite its coconut origin it has a low comedogenicity rating, unlike raw coconut oil.
A water-based coconut extract (distinct from coconut oil) used for light hydration and skin-conditioning benefits.
Coconut water used as a light, electrolyte- and humectant-rich hydrating base liquid in mists and gels.

Refined coconut-derived oil used in some formulas as an emollient. Highly comedogenic on its own and best avoided by acne-prone or fungal-acne-prone users; note this is the same plant source as Coconut Oil but listed under its alternate INCI form.
Coffee bean extract rich in chlorogenic acids and caffeine, used for antioxidant and mild brightening/de-puffing effects; generally gentle and well tolerated.
An emollient oil pressed from coffee beans, containing antioxidant compounds; functions mainly as a moisturizing carrier oil with light antioxidant support.
Myrrh essential oil used for fragrance and traditional skin-conditioning claims, but as an essential oil it carries a real risk of irritation and sensitization.
pregnancy_notes: Myrrh has traditional associations with uterine stimulation; best avoided during pregnancy.

what_it_does: Carnauba wax — a hard plant wax used to firm up the texture of sticks and balms; functions mainly as a texture agent rather than a skin-benefit ingredient.
A copper salt that supplies copper ions used in some formulas to support collagen-related enzyme activity and skin repair processes.
pregnancy_notes: Limited safety data in pregnancy for topical copper peptide/salt formulas; consult a doctor if used in high concentration.

what_it_does: A copper-bound tripeptide (GHK-Cu) that supports wound healing, collagen remodeling, and antioxidant defense; can have a distinctive blue color in concentrated formulas and may cause irritation in some users.
pregnancy_notes: No specific human pregnancy safety data exists; generally advised to use with caution or avoid given the lack of dedicated studies.

what_it_does: A newer copper-bound peptide complex marketed for collagen support and skin remodeling similar to Copper Tripeptide-1 but engineered for improved stability.
pregnancy_notes: No dedicated human pregnancy safety data exists; cautious approach generally recommended.

what_it_does: Cordyceps mushroom extract used in anti-aging skincare for its reputed revitalizing and antioxidant properties.
A sweet, hay-like fragrance compound naturally found in tonka bean and lavender, used widely to add warmth to fragrance blends.
pregnancy_notes: No specific pregnancy contraindication, but a recognized contact allergen.

what_it_does: Crambe seed oil — a stable, lightweight emollient oil valued in formulation for its long shelf life and silky finish.
Cucumber fruit extract that hydrates and lightly soothes skin, commonly used in cooling gels and eye products for its refreshing feel.
Pumpkin fruit extract supplying natural enzymes and AHAs for mild exfoliation, plus vitamin A precursors and antioxidants.
Pumpkin seed oil — a moderate-weight oil with antioxidant compounds, used to soften and nourish dry skin.
Turmeric root extract used for its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory polyphenols, helping soothe skin and support an even tone; the bright pigment can stain fabrics and lighter skin temporarily.

The active antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compound found in turmeric; helps calm irritation and provides some brightening benefit, though its strong yellow pigment can temporarily stain skin in high concentrations.
A volatile silicone fluid similar to cyclopentasiloxane that evaporates quickly, leaving a smooth, non-greasy after-feel and helping other ingredients spread evenly.
A lightweight, volatile silicone that evaporates after application, giving a silky slip and smooth finish without heavy residue. Used widely in primers, sunscreens, and serums.
Carrot root extract rich in beta-carotene and antioxidants, used to support skin radiance and provide mild antioxidant protection.
Carrot seed oil — a richly colored oil with carotenoid antioxidants, used in small amounts for its nourishing and mild protective properties.
A brightening peptide marketed to interrupt the pigment-transfer pathway between melanocytes and skin cells, used as a niacinamide-adjacent alternative for tone evening.
pregnancy_notes: No dedicated pregnancy safety data; general caution applied to novel peptides.

what_it_does: A plant-derived, sugar-based gentle surfactant that cleanses without stripping the skin barrier, a favorite in sulfate-free and sensitive-skin formulas.
A synthetic ester emollient that mimics the feel of natural oils, giving products a smooth, non-greasy texture.
A plant-derived glycol that helps boost preservative effectiveness while also softening skin texture, used in many 'natural-leaning' modern formulas. Gentle and low-irritation.

An organic-acid preservative effective mainly against mold and fungi, often combined with benzyl alcohol in preservative blends. Generally well tolerated, though it can be sensitizing for a small number of people.
A fine powder made of fossilized algae, used as a gentle physical exfoliant and oil-absorbing ingredient in masks and scrubs.

A formaldehyde-releasing preservative that slowly liberates small amounts of formaldehyde to keep bacteria and fungi from growing. Effective, but a real concern for people with formaldehyde sensitivity since it can trigger allergic contact dermatitis.
pregnancy_notes: Formaldehyde-releasing preservatives are generally considered low-risk at cosmetic use levels, but many people prefer to avoid them during pregnancy and if they have sensitive or allergy-prone skin.

what_it_does: A very lightweight, fast-spreading emollient that gives a dry, non-greasy finish, often used as a silicone alternative in modern lightweight moisturizers.
A lightweight, silky emollient with a dry finish, used to improve spreadability without leaving a heavy or greasy residue.

A pH-adjusting and emulsifying ingredient that has fallen out of favor because it can react with certain preservatives to form nitrosamines, compounds with cancer concerns. Largely phased out of modern formulas in favor of safer pH adjusters.
pregnancy_notes: Potential nitrosamine-formation risk when combined with certain other ingredients; best avoided, especially during pregnancy.

what_it_does: A photostable chemical UVA filter that helps protect skin from UVA-driven photoaging and also stabilizes other filters like avobenzone in a formula.
An oil-soluble UVB filter prized for being one of the most photostable chemical sunscreen actives available, holding up well under prolonged sun exposure.
An emollient solvent commonly used to help dissolve and disperse other UV filters evenly through a sunscreen formula, improving spreadability and SPF performance.
A lightweight, plant-derived synthetic ester emollient used as a silicone alternative for a smooth, dry-touch finish.
A lightweight ester emollient that gives a dry, silky after-feel and helps actives like sunscreen filters spread evenly without a greasy residue.
Mainly a lightweight emollient and solvent, but also functions as a carrier that helps disperse organic UV filters evenly across the skin in sunscreen formulas.
A silicone that smooths skin, fills fine lines, and forms a breathable barrier to reduce water loss. Gives products a silky slip. Non-comedogenic for most.
A water-dispersible silicone derivative used as an emulsifier and conditioning agent that bridges silicone and water phases for a smooth, silky finish.
A silicone elastomer that gels other silicone fluids to create a soft, velvety, blurring texture often used in primers and silicone-heavy sunscreens.
A silicone-based emulsifier and mild surfactant that helps stabilize sunscreen and skincare emulsions while providing a light, silky after-feel.
A silicone-based emulsifier that combines silicone slip with meadowfoam oil derivatives to help stabilize water-and-oil mixtures and add a light, silky finish.
A silicone elastomer gel used to thicken silicone-rich formulas and create a soft-focus, blurred finish on skin, popular in pore-blurring primers.
A higher-molecular-weight silicone related to dimethicone that forms a breathable, flexible film, smoothing the skin's surface and adding a soft, silky feel.
A synthetic peptide marketed as a topical alternative to injectable neuromodulators, intended to relax facial muscle micro-contractions and soften expression lines.
pregnancy_notes: No dedicated pregnancy safety data; general caution applied to novel neuropeptides during pregnancy.

what_it_does: A salt form of glycyrrhizic acid (from licorice) used for its soothing, anti-inflammatory, and mild brightening properties; very gentle and a common addition to calming or redness-targeting formulas.
A stable, water-soluble vitamin C derivative used for antioxidant and brightening benefits with good formulation stability and gentle tolerance.
A mild amphoteric cleansing surfactant derived from coconut, used to reduce irritation and boost lather in gentle face and body washes.
A mild amphoteric secondary surfactant used to reduce irritation and round out the foam profile of gentle cleansers, similar in role to cocoamphodiacetate.
A mild, amino-acid-based surfactant derived from coconut and glutamic acid, used for gentle, low-irritation cleansing in sensitive-skin formulas.
A chelating agent that binds metal ions to keep formulas stable and preservatives effective. Used in tiny amounts; inert on skin.
A mild sulfosuccinate surfactant that cleanses gently and adds a light, fluffy lather, often combined with other gentle surfactants to reduce overall irritation.
A mild sulfosuccinate surfactant that produces light, fluffy foam while being gentler than traditional sulfates, often used in baby and sensitive-skin cleansers.
A pH-buffering salt used to maintain a stable acidity level in a formula. Generally non-irritating and considered a safe, inert functional ingredient.

A formaldehyde-releasing preservative, one of the most widely used in shampoos and rinse-off products. EU regulation caps its concentration, and people with formaldehyde allergy or sensitive skin can react to it.
pregnancy_notes: Considered low-risk at regulated cosmetic concentrations, but its formaldehyde-releasing nature leads many people to avoid it during pregnancy as a precaution.

what_it_does: A silicone-based broad-spectrum UV filter that absorbs both UVA and UVB rays while leaving a silky, non-greasy after-feel on skin.

The free-acid form of the chelating agent ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid, used less often directly than its sodium salts but functions the same way: binding metal ions to stabilize formulas and boost preservative efficacy.
A polyphenol antioxidant found in berries and pomegranate that helps brighten skin tone and protect against UV-induced pigmentation when used alongside sun protection.
Horsetail plant extract containing silica and antioxidant compounds, used for mild astringent and skin-conditioning benefits; generally gentle.
A naturally derived amino-acid antioxidant (often from mushroom fermentation) gaining popularity for its strong free-radical scavenging ability and skin-protective effects; gentle and well tolerated.
A lightweight ester derived from linoleic acid, used as an emollient that suits oily or acne-prone skin better than oleic-acid-derived alternatives.
A chemical UVB filter from the PABA family; once popular but now used sparingly due to a relatively high incidence of allergic and photoallergic skin reactions.
pregnancy_notes: Known sensitizer; many formulators avoid it generally, and data specific to pregnancy is limited, so caution is advised.

what_it_does: An oil-soluble ester of ferulic acid used as an antioxidant and UV-protective adjunct that can help stabilize formulas and protect against free radicals; generally gentle.
One of the most widely used chemical UVB filters worldwide, valued for its light texture, though it can degrade somewhat in sunlight and is often paired with photostabilizers.
pregnancy_notes: Some endocrine-activity signals seen in animal studies at high doses; considered low-risk topically at regulated concentrations but a few prenatal-care guides suggest alternatives where available.

what_it_does: A common synthetic emollient ester that gives lotions a smooth, light texture. Considered low comedogenicity compared to isopropyl palmitate.
A chemical UVB filter that absorbs short-wave UV rays; also used at low levels as a solubilizer for other sunscreen actives.
A gentle preservative-booster and skin-conditioning agent that lets formulas use less of harsher preservatives. Well tolerated.
A paraben preservative very similar to methylparaben, used to stop microbial growth and extend shelf life. Considered one of the gentler, lower-concern parabens by cosmetic chemists.
A chelating agent that binds metal ions, used as an alternative to EDTA, particularly to prevent discoloration and rancidity in formulas. Well tolerated at the low concentrations typically used.
Eucalyptus essential oil with a strong scent and mild antimicrobial properties, but it carries real sensitization and irritation risk on skin.
pregnancy_notes: Eucalyptus oil is often advised against during pregnancy due to limited safety data and irritation risk.

what_it_does: A clove-scented fragrance compound naturally found in clove, cinnamon, and basil oils, used to add a spicy note to perfumed cosmetic products.
pregnancy_notes: No specific pregnancy contraindication, but a known sensitizer at higher concentrations.

what_it_does: Candelilla wax — a vegan plant wax used as a thickener and structuring agent in balms, sticks, and lip products.
A natural extract of treemoss lichen used to add a woody, mossy fragrance note to perfumes; a well-documented contact allergen.
pregnancy_notes: No specific pregnancy contraindication, but a strong known natural fragrance sensitizer; usage is restricted in the EU.

what_it_does: A natural extract of oakmoss lichen used to add an earthy, mossy fragrance note to perfumes; one of the most potent natural fragrance allergens.
pregnancy_notes: No specific pregnancy contraindication, but one of the strongest known natural fragrance sensitizers; usage is restricted in the EU.

what_it_does: A floral-scented fragrance ingredient also used as a deodorizing agent that helps inhibit odor-causing bacteria on skin.
pregnancy_notes: No specific pregnancy contraindication, but best minimized in sensitized skin.

what_it_does: A plant-derived antioxidant that helps stabilize and boost the effectiveness of vitamin C formulations while offering its own protection against environmental free-radical damage.

A potent antimicrobial preservative now banned from cosmetics in the EU and heavily restricted elsewhere due to its classification as a human carcinogen and strong skin sensitizer. Should not appear as a direct ingredient in modern products; formaldehyde-releasing preservatives are used instead.
pregnancy_notes: Classified as a human carcinogen with strong sensitizing potential; direct use is banned in EU cosmetics and should be avoided entirely.

what_it_does: An organic acid used in small amounts as a pH adjuster, sometimes also valued for mild preservative-boosting properties. Low irritation potential at use levels.

A yeast ferment filtrate used for hydrating, brightening, and antioxidant benefits, popularized in 'essence' style products; generally gentle though ferment filtrates can rarely sensitize reactive skin.
Reishi mushroom extract valued for antioxidant polysaccharides and beta-glucans that help soothe and condition skin.
A naturally occurring fragrance ingredient with a rose-like scent, found in essential oils like rose and citronella and widely used to scent skincare and haircare products.
pregnancy_notes: No specific pregnancy contraindication, but fragrance allergens are best minimized if skin is sensitized.

what_it_does: An antioxidant-rich botanical extract known for supporting microcirculation and protecting skin against environmental stressors; generally well tolerated.

A polyhydroxy acid (PHA) that exfoliates gently while also acting as a humectant and antioxidant; its larger molecule size makes it less irritating than AHAs, suiting sensitive and rosacea-prone skin.

A strong antimicrobial preservative occasionally used in cosmetics and more commonly in industrial/medical disinfectant contexts. Known to be a significant skin and respiratory sensitizer, so it's rarely used in modern leave-on skincare.
pregnancy_notes: Strong sensitizer with respiratory irritation potential; not appropriate for routine cosmetic use, and best avoided during pregnancy.

what_it_does: A potent antioxidant tripeptide naturally produced by the body, used topically for brightening and antioxidant support; oral/IV use for skin lightening is more controversial and not well regulated, but topical cosmetic use is generally considered gentle.
pregnancy_notes: Topical cosmetic use has limited dedicated pregnancy data; injectable/oral glutathione for skin whitening is unrelated and not recommended during pregnancy. Caution is reasonable given limited data.

what_it_does: A mild emollient ester with some preservative-boosting properties, used to support natural-leaning preservative systems while also softening skin feel. Gentle and rarely irritating.
A superstar humectant that pulls water into the skin and helps maintain the moisture barrier. Well tolerated by virtually all skin types.
Similar to glyceryl caprylate, a glycerin ester with mild antimicrobial action used to help preserve formulas, often combined with other naturally-derived preservative boosters. Gentle on skin.
A multitasking ester that acts as an emollient and mild emulsifier while also providing some antimicrobial benefit that can boost preservative systems.
A multifunctional emollient ester with mild antimicrobial activity, often used to boost preservation while softening skin.
A plant-derived humectant that helps skin cells regulate their own water balance, providing long-lasting hydration even in low-humidity conditions.
A lightweight emollient ester rich in linoleic acid, used to soften skin and support the barrier without heaviness.
An emollient ester and mild emulsifier derived from glycerin and oleic acid, used to soften formulas and help blend oil and water phases.
A fatty emulsifier and emollient that binds oil and water into smooth creams while softening skin. Common and gentle.
A naturally derived, vegetable-based emulsifier that creates stable, lightweight oil-in-water emulsions and is popular in 'clean' formulations.
A self-emulsifying form of glyceryl stearate that includes a small amount of soap to help it form creamy, stable oil-in-water emulsions on its own.
A lightweight ester used as an emollient and solvent, helping other ingredients spread evenly across the skin.

A glycerin ester of undecylenic acid (derived from castor oil) with antifungal and antimicrobial properties, used in 'natural' preservative systems and some anti-dandruff or anti-acne formulas. Generally well tolerated.
A simple amino acid sometimes used as a pH buffer and mild humectant, also found in some 'plumping' or peptide-adjacent claims. Essentially non-irritating.
Soybean extract containing isoflavones with antioxidant and mild brightening properties; generally gentle though soy allergy is a rare consideration for sensitive individuals.
Soybean oil used as an emollient with mild antioxidant tocopherol content; functions mainly as a moisturizing carrier oil rather than a targeted active.
A pearlizing and thickening agent derived from fatty acids, used to give shampoos, body washes, and lotions an opaque, pearly sheen and richer feel.
A pearlescent, opacifying agent that also adds emolliency and a creamy texture to lotions and cleansers.
The smallest AHA, derived from sugar cane. Exfoliates the surface to smooth texture, brighten, and reduce fine lines. Can increase sun sensitivity.
A small dipeptide used as a skin-conditioning and humectant-adjacent ingredient, sometimes paired with brightening or anti-aging actives to support overall skin texture.
A licorice-root-derived anti-inflammatory and brightening acid that soothes redness while helping even skin tone; well tolerated and rarely irritating.

Licorice root extract containing glabridin and other compounds that inhibit tyrosinase, offering brightening and soothing, anti-inflammatory benefits; very gentle and suitable for sensitive or redness-prone skin.
Witch hazel extract with tannins that have astringent and antioxidant properties, but the non-distilled form can also contain irritating volatile compounds.
Distilled witch hazel water used as an astringent toning ingredient; versions with added alcohol can be drying and irritating to sensitive skin.
pregnancy_notes: Topical use is generally fine, but alcohol-containing versions can be drying.

what_it_does: A natural clay mineral used to thicken and stabilize formulas, especially mineral sunscreens and masks, by suspending particles and adding body.
Sunflower oil — a lightweight, linoleic-acid-rich oil that supports the skin barrier and is well tolerated by acne-prone skin compared to oleic-acid-heavy oils.
A peptide derived from yeast extract believed to stimulate skin renewal and support a more even-looking, firmer complexion in anti-aging formulas.
pregnancy_notes: No dedicated pregnancy safety data; treated with general caution common to novel peptide actives.

what_it_does: A synthetic fragrance ingredient with a jasmine-like floral scent, one of the most common allergens flagged in fragranced cosmetics.
pregnancy_notes: No specific pregnancy contraindication, but a recognized contact allergen.

what_it_does: A fatty alcohol emollient with a light, non-greasy feel that improves spreadability in lotions and creams.
A botanical source of natural AHAs that provides mild exfoliation and antioxidant benefits, often marketed as a 'natural BHA' alternative though weaker in effect.
Sea buckthorn fruit oil — a deep orange, carotenoid-rich oil prized for soothing and supporting compromised skin, typically used in small amounts within a blend due to its strong color and scent.
pregnancy_notes: Limited topical safety data; generally considered fine in cosmetic amounts, but check with your doctor.

what_it_does: A chemical UVB filter that helps absorb and dissipate UV radiation before it damages skin cells, commonly used to boost SPF in combination formulas.
pregnancy_notes: EU's SCCS flagged potential endocrine-disrupting properties at high concentrations and lowered the maximum allowed concentration; topical use in approved limits is still considered low-risk, but some clinicians suggest minimizing exposure during pregnancy.

what_it_does: A natural humectant with mild antibacterial and soothing properties, often used in cleansers and masks for hydration and a calming effect.
Extract derived from honey that humectant-hydrates skin and offers mild antibacterial and soothing properties, often used in masks and cleansers.
A traditional East Asian herb extract used in modern skincare for its calming and purifying properties on blemish-prone, sensitive skin.
A natural humectant and skin component that binds water to keep skin hydrated and plump. Gentle and suitable for all skin types.
A fine mineral powder used as a gentle physical exfoliant in scrubs and toothpaste, and as a mattifying, oil-absorbing agent in skincare and makeup.
A waxy, hydrogenated form of castor oil used as a thickener and stabilizer in lipsticks, balms, and creams.
A more stable, oxidation-resistant version of lecithin used as an emulsifier and skin-conditioning agent in creams and serums.
A lightweight, silky synthetic emollient that improves spreadability and gives products a non-greasy, silicone-like skin feel.
A synthetic, silicone-like emollient that gives a smooth, non-greasy finish and is often used as a lightweight alternative to mineral oil. Essentially non-comedogenic.
A lower-molecular-weight form of hyaluronic acid that can penetrate slightly deeper into the upper skin layers, providing hydration with a lightweight feel.

A potent prescription-strength skin-lightening agent that inhibits melanin production to treat severe hyperpigmentation; effective but carries risks of irritation, sensitization, and (with long-term/high-dose misuse) exogenous ochronosis, so it's tightly regulated and banned or restricted in many countries' OTC cosmetics.
pregnancy_notes: Generally advised against during pregnancy due to relatively high dermal absorption and insufficient safety data; most obstetric and dermatology guidance recommends avoiding it.

what_it_does: A preservative-boosting and antioxidant ingredient derived from plant sources, often used to help reduce the total amount of traditional preservative needed in a formula. Generally gentle but can be mildly sensitizing for a small number of users.
A synthetic fragrance ingredient with a lily-of-the-valley scent used in perfumes and scented personal care products.
pregnancy_notes: No specific pregnancy contraindication, but best minimized in sensitized skin.

what_it_does: A modern, electrolyte-tolerant thickener that gels formulas with a light, cushiony, silicone-like skin feel, often paired with polyglyceryl-4 caprate as a stabilizer pair.
A plant-derived cellulose gum that thickens water-based formulas and creates smooth, often slightly stringy gel textures in toners and serums.
A synthetic floral fragrance compound formerly common in perfumes; banned from use in EU cosmetic products since 2022 due to its strong sensitizing potential.
pregnancy_notes: Banned in EU cosmetics since August 2021/2022 due to sensitization risk; should not appear in products, but is flagged if found in older or non-EU formulas.

what_it_does: A non-irritating ester form of retinoic acid that binds retinoid receptors directly without needing skin conversion, giving mild retinoid-like benefits with much less irritation than retinol or tretinoin.
pregnancy_notes: Part of the retinoid family; despite its mildness, dermatologists generally advise avoiding all retinoids during pregnancy as a precaution.

what_it_does: A plant-derived polysaccharide thickener from guar beans, used to add viscosity and a smooth slip to gels, conditioners, and cleansers.
A cellulose-derived thickener and film-former that adds viscosity and a smooth, slightly cushiony skin feel to gels and lotions.
A non-retinoid 'retinol alternative' molecule (marketed as Granactive-adjacent and used in pregnancy-marketed lines) that signals similar anti-aging pathways without classic retinoid irritation.
pregnancy_notes: Not a true vitamin A retinoid, so it is often marketed as pregnancy-friendly, but as a newer ingredient with limited long-term human safety data, many clinicians still suggest caution.

what_it_does: A synthetic antioxidant structurally related to CoQ10 that was popularized for anti-aging serums; provides strong free-radical protection, though formulation stability and irritation can vary by product.
pregnancy_notes: Limited dedicated safety data for use during pregnancy; not well studied, so caution is generally advised.

what_it_does: A formaldehyde-releasing preservative similar to diazolidinyl urea, used to extend shelf life by slowly releasing formaldehyde. Can be sensitizing, particularly for people already allergic to formaldehyde.
pregnancy_notes: Same formaldehyde-release caveats as other members of this preservative class.

what_it_does: A sugar-alcohol that acts as a mild humectant and is often included in formulas alongside other moisture-binding ingredients.

A broad-spectrum preservative often used in small amounts alongside other preservatives, effective against fungi and bacteria. EU regulation restricts its concentration, especially in body lotion and other widely-applied products, due to sensitization concerns.
pregnancy_notes: Regulatory bodies cap concentration due to allergy potential; reasonable to be cautious with leave-on products containing it.

what_it_does: A lightweight chemical UVB filter, similar in action to ethylhexyl methoxycinnamate, used to boost broad-spectrum protection with a less greasy feel.

A branched-chain paraben preservative used less frequently than methyl- or propylparaben, with a similar but somewhat stronger estrogenic profile that puts it under the same EU restrictions as propyl- and butylparaben.
pregnancy_notes: Grouped with the longer-chain parabens of greater endocrine-activity concern; reasonable to limit exposure during pregnancy.

what_it_does: A nonionic solubilizer used to help blend oils and fragrance into water-based formulas, especially in toners and lightweight essences.
A rich ester emollient that gives a velvety, cushioned skin feel, often used in body butters and rich night creams.
A light, fast-absorbing synthetic ester used as an emollient to improve spreadability and reduce greasiness in lotions and sunscreens.
A lightweight, volatile silicone-like hydrocarbon solvent that evaporates quickly, leaving a dry, non-greasy finish; frequently used in long-wear makeup and sunscreen.
A clove-and-carnation-scented fragrance compound related to eugenol, used to add spicy floral notes to perfumed products.
pregnancy_notes: No specific pregnancy contraindication, but a strong known sensitizer; best avoided by reactive skin.

what_it_does: A lightweight synthetic ester emollient with a silky, non-greasy finish, commonly used in makeup and lightweight moisturizers as an alternative to mineral oil.
A lightweight synthetic emollient ester that improves spreadability and gives a silky finish, with a lower comedogenic risk than isopropyl palmitate.
A lightweight emollient and texture enhancer that gives a silky feel, but is notably comedogenic and best avoided by breakout-prone skin.
A synthetic emollient ester that gives a smooth, fast-absorbing skin feel. Well known in the cosmetic-science community for being notably comedogenic — worth avoiding if you're acne-prone.
A branched-chain paraben preservative, chemically similar to propylparaben, used to inhibit microbial growth. Subject to the same EU restrictions as the other longer-chain parabens.
pregnancy_notes: Grouped with longer-chain parabens of greater endocrine-activity concern.

what_it_does: A rich, cushiony ester emollient used in creams and makeup to improve slip and provide a soft, smooth after-feel.
A potent oral or topical retinoic acid isomer used medically for severe acne; topical forms are far less common than the oral prescription medication.
pregnancy_notes: Oral isotretinoin is a well-documented severe teratogen requiring strict pregnancy prevention programs; topical forms are also avoided in pregnancy as a precaution.

what_it_does: Jasmine essential oil used for its fragrance, with known potential to cause allergic contact reactions in fragrance-sensitive skin.
A gentle white clay that absorbs excess oil and impurities, commonly used in masks and powders for mattifying and mild cleansing benefits.
A fungal-derived brightening acid that inhibits tyrosinase to fade dark spots and hyperpigmentation; can be unstable in formulas and occasionally sensitizing.
pregnancy_notes: Limited dedicated pregnancy safety data; many prenatal skincare guides suggest caution or substituting better-studied brighteners like vitamin C or niacinamide during pregnancy.

what_it_does: A more stable, lipid-soluble ester of kojic acid designed to reduce the irritation and discoloration issues of pure kojic acid while retaining brightening, tyrosinase-inhibiting activity.
pregnancy_notes: As a kojic acid derivative, the same precautionary guidance applies; limited dedicated pregnancy safety data.

what_it_does: A gentle AHA that exfoliates while also hydrating, making it a good entry point for sensitive skin. Improves tone, texture, and mild pigmentation.
A probiotic-derived ferment ingredient used to support the skin barrier and microbiome balance while offering antioxidant and soothing benefits; generally gentle.
A gentle polyhydroxy acid (PHA) that exfoliates and provides antioxidant and humectant benefits, well tolerated even by reactive or rosacea-prone skin.
Brown algae (kelp) extract rich in minerals and polysaccharides that hydrates and conditions skin while offering mild antioxidant support.
A rich, sheep-wool-derived occlusive emollient excellent for very dry skin and lips. A known, though uncommon, contact allergen for a subset of users.
A foam-stabilizing and thickening surfactant additive, similar to cocamide DEA, used to improve lather quality in cleansing products.
pregnancy_notes: Like other DEA-condensate ingredients, trace nitrosamine formation is a theoretical concern; many brands avoid it as a precaution.

what_it_does: A small nonionic surfactant used mainly as a solubilizer that helps fragrance and oils mix evenly into water-based formulas.
A nonionic surfactant and solubilizer used to help disperse oils and actives evenly into lightweight, water-based serums and toners.
An amino-acid-derived surfactant used for its mild cleansing and emulsifying properties, less commonly seen than its sodium salt form.
A mild amphoteric surfactant used to gently boost foam and reduce the irritation potential of stronger cleansing agents in a wash formula.
A gentle, plant-derived surfactant from the glucoside family that cleanses effectively with low irritation potential, commonly paired with other mild surfactants.
An emollient ester with mild emulsifying properties used to add a smooth, lightweight slip to lotions and creams.
A silicone emulsifier that helps combine water and oil/silicone phases into stable, lightweight, silky-finish formulas, common in modern sunscreens and primers.
A specialized silicone emulsifier used to disperse mineral sunscreen actives evenly in water, helping reduce the white-cast and chalky feel of zinc oxide or titanium dioxide sunscreens.
A non-oil lavender extract used for light fragrance and mild calming properties; gentler than the essential oil but can still trigger sensitivities in fragrance-allergic skin.
Lavender essential oil prized for fragrance and a mild calming scent, but it is a known fragrance allergen that can sensitize skin with repeated use.
pregnancy_notes: Essential oils are generally used cautiously and in low amounts during pregnancy.

what_it_does: A naturally derived phospholipid emulsifier and emollient that also supports the skin barrier; sourced from soy, sunflower, or egg.
A processed form of lecithin used as a mild emulsifier and skin-conditioning agent, similar in role to standard lecithin but with different emulsifying strength.
Meadowfoam seed oil — a highly stable, lightweight oil with an unusually long shelf life thanks to its unique fatty acid profile; gives a smooth, non-greasy finish.
A fragrant compound found in citrus oils, used for scent. One of the EU's 26 declarable fragrance allergens; can oxidize and irritate sensitive skin.
A naturally occurring fragrant alcohol (lavender, citrus). A declarable EU fragrance allergen that can oxidize into sensitizing compounds.
An essential fatty acid that supports the skin barrier and is often deficient in acne-prone skin; used in lightweight oils and serums to help balance sebum composition and reduce comedones.
Flaxseed oil — rich in omega-3 fatty acids and used as a barrier-supportive emollient, though it oxidizes easily and has a notable scent.
A potent antioxidant that helps neutralize free radicals and may support collagen-related skin repair; can be unstable and occasionally irritating in higher concentrations.
Honeysuckle flower extract with antioxidant and mild antibacterial properties, sometimes used as a natural preservative booster as well as a soothing botanical; generally gentle.

A plant-derived (Helichrysum stoechas) brightening active marketed for inhibiting melanin transfer to reduce dark spots and pigmentation; generally well tolerated with limited but favorable safety data.
pregnancy_notes: Newer ingredient with limited dedicated pregnancy safety research; caution recommended pending more data.

what_it_does: Macadamia oil — rich in palmitoleic acid, which closely resembles a component of human sebum, making it a well-absorbed emollient especially suited to mature skin.
A clay-derived mineral thickener that helps stabilize emulsions and gives lotions and creams a smooth, gel-like texture.

A stable, water-soluble vitamin C ester that provides antioxidant and brightening effects; gentler than pure ascorbic acid but generally considered to penetrate and convert less efficiently than some newer derivatives.
A fatty acid salt used as a texturizer and anti-caking agent in powders and pressed cosmetics to improve smoothness and slip.
An AHA derived from apples, often used in blends to adjust pH or boost the exfoliating effect of other acids; rarely used alone at high strength.
pregnancy_notes: Limited specific safety data; treated like other AHAs and generally advised in moderation during pregnancy.

what_it_does: A next-generation PHA that exfoliates gently while binding moisture and supporting the skin barrier, often marketed as a soothing alternative to AHAs.
Apple stem cell extract marketed for antioxidant and anti-aging benefits, based on research into plant stem cells' protective compounds.
Mallow flower extract rich in mucilage, valued for its softening and soothing effect on dry or irritated skin.
A large-molecule AHA derived from bitter almonds that exfoliates slowly and gently due to its size, making it a good option for sensitive or melanin-rich skin prone to post-inflammatory pigmentation.
pregnancy_notes: Considered one of the gentler AHAs and is often recommended over glycolic acid in pregnancy, but as with all leave-on acids most clinicians advise low concentrations and checking with a provider.

what_it_does: Tea tree essential oil with antimicrobial and anti-acne properties, but a known contact allergen and sensitizer, especially when oxidized or used at high concentration.
pregnancy_notes: Limited data on topical essential oil use in pregnancy; use diluted and in moderation.

what_it_does: Peppermint leaf extract that gives a cooling sensation, but the active menthol-related compounds can be irritating, especially for sensitive or compromised skin.
pregnancy_notes: Generally fine in low leave-on amounts, but minty/cooling actives are best used cautiously.

what_it_does: Peppermint essential oil that creates a strong cooling sensation; a well-documented skin irritant and sensitizer when used undiluted or in high amounts.
pregnancy_notes: Essential oils should be used sparingly and diluted; consult a doctor before use during pregnancy.

what_it_does: A compound derived from mint that creates a cooling, tingling sensation on skin. Used in small amounts for a refreshing feel, but can be irritating and sensitizing at higher levels.
pregnancy_notes: No specific pregnancy contraindication for topical use in normal cosmetic amounts, but can irritate sensitive or compromised skin.

what_it_does: A silicone polymer related to dimethicone that forms a smooth, breathable film on skin, providing a silky after-feel and a light protective barrier.
A synthetic fragrance ingredient with a violet-leaf, fruity scent, used in small amounts in fine fragrance and perfumed cosmetics.
pregnancy_notes: No specific pregnancy contraindication, but a potent known sensitizer even at low concentrations.

what_it_does: A naturally derived, sugar-based co-emulsifier that helps stabilize creams and lotions, commonly paired with PEG-20 methyl glucose sesquistearate.
A wintergreen-scented compound related to salicylic acid, used as a fragrance and cooling/warming agent in topical treatments such as muscle balms and some skincare.
pregnancy_notes: Structurally related to salicylates; large or frequent topical use is best avoided in pregnancy, particularly in the third trimester.

what_it_does: Often used together with methylisothiazolinone as a preservative duo (MCI/MI) effective against a broad range of microbes. Has a well-documented contact-allergy risk and is restricted to rinse-off products in the EU.
pregnancy_notes: Same sensitization concerns as methylisothiazolinone; best avoided by anyone with a known isothiazolinone allergy.

what_it_does: A preservative once common in cosmetics that was banned from leave-on products and later from all cosmetics in the EU after being identified as a significant contact allergen.
pregnancy_notes: Banned in EU cosmetics due to high sensitization rates; should not appear in modern formulas, but worth flagging if found in older or non-compliant products.

what_it_does: A broad-spectrum organic UV filter that behaves like a hybrid mineral-organic particle, sitting on the skin's surface to scatter and absorb UV rays with very low irritation potential. Not approved for use in the US as of this writing.

A fast-acting synthetic preservative against bacteria and fungi that became a major cause of contact-allergy outbreaks when used in leave-on products at higher concentrations during the 2010s. Now restricted to rinse-off products in the EU and considered a real allergen risk by dermatologists.
pregnancy_notes: Not specifically dangerous in pregnancy, but its high rate of skin sensitization makes it worth avoiding for anyone with sensitive or reactive skin, pregnant or not.

what_it_does: A widely used paraben preservative that prevents bacteria and mold from growing in a formula. It has one of the longest safety track records of any cosmetic preservative, with CIR review finding it non-irritating and non-sensitizing at use levels, though it remains a target of consumer concern over weak estrogenic activity.
A naturally occurring mineral ground into fine particles that gives makeup and skincare products a shimmery or pearlescent finish.

A purified, highly refined petroleum-derived occlusive that locks in moisture extremely effectively. Despite its reputation, cosmetic-grade mineral oil is non-comedogenic and very well tolerated, including by sensitive and eczema-prone skin.
An acidic pH-buffering salt used to adjust and stabilize formula acidity, often paired with disodium phosphate to fine-tune pH precisely. Low irritation potential.
Moringa seed oil — a lightweight, antioxidant-rich oil that absorbs well and is used for its mild conditioning and protective benefits.
Mulberry root extract that contains tyrosinase-inhibiting compounds and is used as a natural brightening agent to help fade dark spots and even skin tone; generally gentle.
A mulberry species extract used similarly to other mulberry root extracts for its tyrosinase-inhibiting, skin-brightening effect; well tolerated in typical leave-on use.
A fatty acid used as a thickener and surfactant component in cleansers and creams; can be slightly more comedogenic than other fatty acids.
A lipid-conjugated peptide marketed to support lash and brow growth, as well as general skin firmness in some anti-aging formulas.
pregnancy_notes: No dedicated pregnancy safety data; general caution applied to novel peptides.

what_it_does: A mild, plant-derived surfactant from the glucoside family used to cleanse gently and stabilize foam in sulfate-free formulas.
A wax ester emollient that thickens and lubricates formulas, giving a smooth glide. Has a notably higher comedogenicity rating, so it can be problematic for breakout-prone skin.
Lotus flower extract used for its mild antioxidant and skin-conditioning properties, popular in calming and hydrating formulas.
Lotus leaf extract used for light astringent and antioxidant benefits, sometimes included in oil-control or pore-refining products.
