A lightweight synthetic ester emollient prized for its dry, non-tacky finish, commonly used in makeup and modern moisturizers.

The free acid form of vitamin B3, less commonly used than niacinamide in cosmetics; functions similarly as an antioxidant and skin-conditioning agent but can cause more flushing/irritation than niacinamide.
A multitasking form of vitamin B3 that reduces redness, regulates oil, fades hyperpigmentation, and strengthens the skin barrier. One of the best-studied actives.

A vitamin B3 derivative studied mainly for its role in cellular NAD+ production; appears in newer anti-aging formulas as an antioxidant-adjacent ingredient, though topical skin-specific data is still limited.
A peptide marketed for brightening and reducing the appearance of hyperpigmentation by modulating melanin-related signaling pathways.
pregnancy_notes: No dedicated pregnancy safety data exists; cautious approach generally applied to novel peptides.

what_it_does: A chemical UVB filter that also photostabilizes other filters like avobenzone, extending their effectiveness. It has a mild but real allergic-contact-dermatitis profile in a small subset of users.
A synthetic ester emollient that gives a smooth, slightly rich after-feel, used to soften the texture of creams and lotions.
A highly photostable chemical UVB filter that resists breakdown in sunlight, helping sunscreens maintain their labeled SPF over time.
A fatty alcohol emollient that gives products a light, non-greasy slip and helps other ingredients spread evenly. Generally well tolerated.
A synthetic emollient ester that provides a smooth, non-tacky feel and good spreadability, often used in sunscreens and makeup.
An isothiazolinone preservative used at very low levels mainly in rinse-off and industrial products, with similar allergy concerns to other members of this preservative family.
pregnancy_notes: Sensitization risk is the main concern shared by isothiazolinone preservatives.

what_it_does: Evening primrose oil — another GLA-rich oil used to soothe and support the skin barrier, often included in formulas for sensitive or eczema-prone skin.
Olive fruit oil, a heavy emollient rich in oleic acid that softens skin and helps lock in moisture, though it can feel greasy and is more comedogenic for acne-prone skin.
Olive leaf extract rich in oleuropein, a strong antioxidant polyphenol that helps protect skin from oxidative and environmental stress.
A sulfonate-family cleansing surfactant used to produce a rich, dense foam, similar in role to sodium C14-16 olefin sulfonate in body washes and bar cleansers.
A monounsaturated fatty acid that softens and conditions skin, but can be more irritating and pore-clogging than linoleic-acid-rich alternatives, especially for acne-prone users.
A nonionic emulsifier derived from oleyl alcohol that helps blend oil and water phases together in creams and lotions.
A peptide marketed for skin brightening and reducing the look of dark spots by interfering with pigment transfer mechanisms.
pregnancy_notes: No dedicated pregnancy safety data; general caution applied to novel peptides.

what_it_does: Prickly pear cactus extract rich in antioxidants and polysaccharides, used for its hydrating and skin-soothing benefits.
Oregano leaf extract with natural antimicrobial carvacrol, occasionally used in blemish-targeting formulas but can be irritating in higher amounts.
Rice bran oil — a lightweight, antioxidant-rich oil (notably gamma-oryzanol and vitamin E) that absorbs well and softens skin.
Fermented or non-fermented rice bran water (rice water) used as a hydrating, antioxidant-rich base liquid in toners and essences.
Rice bran wax — a plant-derived wax used to thicken and stabilize anhydrous formulas like balms and sticks, similar in function to beeswax but vegan.
Rice extract used for its antioxidant and brightening properties, a long-standing staple in East Asian beauty traditions for evening tone and texture.
A plant-derived organic acid with mild antimicrobial properties, used as a natural-origin preservative booster in some 'clean beauty' formulas. Generally low irritation.
Peony root extract used in traditional and modern formulas for its mild brightening, antioxidant, and skin-calming properties.
A naturally occurring fatty acid found in skin's own lipids, used to thicken and stabilize creams while contributing to a rich, smooth texture.
A combination peptide ingredient marketed to both relax expression lines and support firmness, blending mechanisms from neuropeptides and signal peptides.
pregnancy_notes: No dedicated pregnancy safety data; general caution applied to novel peptide complexes.

what_it_does: A fatty-acid-conjugated amino acid used as a skin-soothing and barrier-supporting ingredient, sometimes marketed for calming sensitized skin alongside actives.
A signal peptide related to the Matrixyl family used to support the skin's collagen-production pathways for a firmer-looking complexion.
pregnancy_notes: No dedicated pregnancy safety data; general caution applied to novel signal peptides.

what_it_does: A lipid-conjugated signaling peptide related to growth factor pathways, used to support collagen and elastin production for firmer-looking skin.
pregnancy_notes: No dedicated pregnancy safety data; generally grouped with other novel peptides for a cautious approach during pregnancy.

what_it_does: A signal peptide that mimics fragments of collagen breakdown to stimulate the skin's own collagen, elastin, and hyaluronic acid production, improving the look of fine lines over consistent use.
A signal peptide that helps modulate inflammatory pathways associated with skin aging and supports the skin's structural proteins; usually combined with Palmitoyl Tripeptide-1 in Matrixyl 3000.
A signal peptide (Pal-GHK) that supports collagen synthesis and wound-healing pathways, commonly paired with Palmitoyl Tetrapeptide-7 in the Matrixyl 3000 complex.
A signal peptide marketed to support visible firmness and texture improvements, used in some anti-aging serums alongside other Matrixyl-family peptides.
pregnancy_notes: No dedicated pregnancy safety data; general caution applied to novel peptides.

what_it_does: A signal peptide engineered to mimic multiple extracellular matrix repair pathways at once, marketed as Matrixyl Synthe'6 for broader anti-aging signaling than earlier Matrixyl peptides.
A lipidated signal peptide related to the Matrixyl family, marketed to support visible firmness and reduce the look of fine lines with continued use.
pregnancy_notes: No dedicated pregnancy safety data; general caution applied to novel signal peptides.

what_it_does: A signal peptide used in eye-area and firming formulas to support visible skin structure improvements alongside other Matrixyl-family peptides.
pregnancy_notes: No dedicated pregnancy safety data; general caution applied to novel signal peptides.

what_it_does: Ginseng root extract containing ginsenosides with antioxidant and skin-revitalizing properties, often included in anti-aging formulas; generally gentle.
Provitamin B5 — a humectant and soothing agent that hydrates, calms irritation, and supports barrier repair. Gentle and broadly compatible.
A proteolytic enzyme derived from papaya that digests dead surface skin cells (keratin) for a gentler, non-acid form of exfoliation; can be sensitizing for some, especially around the eyes.
A catch-all term for fragrance — a blend of undisclosed aromatic compounds. A leading cause of contact allergy and irritation; best avoided for sensitive or reactive skin.
Passionfruit seed oil — a light, linoleic-acid-rich oil that absorbs quickly and suits combination or oily skin better than heavier oils.
A silicone surfactant that helps blend water and silicone phases together, commonly used to stabilize lightweight, silky lotions and sunscreens.
A widely used PEG-based co-emulsifier that helps create stable, creamy oil-in-water lotions, usually paired with glyceryl stearate.
A PEG-based thickener and emulsion stabilizer that adds viscosity to lotions and cleansers, giving products a richer, more substantial feel.
A water-soluble, sugar-derived emulsifier typically combined with methyl glucose sesquistearate to form a complete, low-irritation emulsifying system.
A PEG-based nonionic emulsifier that helps blend oil and water phases in creams and lotions for a smooth, stable texture.
A water-soluble derivative of PABA used historically as a UVB filter; largely replaced by newer filters due to higher rates of allergic skin reactions associated with PABA chemistry.
pregnancy_notes: PABA-class filters have a documented history of contact allergy and photoallergy; not specifically contraindicated in pregnancy but rarely used in current formulations.

what_it_does: A widely used PEG-based solubilizer that helps disperse oils, fragrance, and essential oils evenly into water-based toners, essences, and serums.
A PEG-based emulsifier and solubilizer that helps blend oils into water and can also boost the clarity of surfactant-based cleansers.
A nonionic solubilizer derived from castor oil that helps blend fragrance and oils into clear, water-based formulas like essences and micellar waters.
A mild, coconut-derived nonionic surfactant used to soften the harshness of stronger cleansing agents and add a conditioning, skin-friendly feel.
A coconut-derived nonionic surfactant used as a mild solubilizer and foam booster, often added to gentle face and body cleansers.
A high-molecular-weight polyethylene glycol used as a humectant and viscosity-builder in water-based formulas, helping retain moisture and adjust thickness.
A small, water-soluble polyethylene glycol used mainly as a humectant and solvent that helps other ingredients dissolve and blend smoothly.
A high-molecular-weight PEG polymer used mainly as a thickener and stabilizer in cleansers and water-based gels.
A silicone surfactant used to blend water and oil/silicone phases smoothly, contributing to the light, silky finish typical of modern sunscreen formulas.
Geranium essential oil used mainly for fragrance, with a real risk of contact sensitization and irritation, particularly with repeated use.
pregnancy_notes: Essential oils carry limited pregnancy safety data; use sparingly and consult a doctor.

what_it_does: A thick, cushiony ester emollient used in rich creams and makeup for a luxurious, velvety texture.
A muscle-relaxing peptide similar in concept to Acetyl Hexapeptide-8, designed to soften the appearance of expression lines by reducing micro-contractions.
pregnancy_notes: Limited safety data specific to pregnancy; treated with the same general caution as other novel neuropeptides.

what_it_does: A multitasking glycol that humectants the skin, helps dissolve other ingredients, and boosts the effectiveness of preservatives. Lighter feeling than propylene glycol.
Avocado oil — a rich, heavy emollient packed with fatty acids and vitamins that deeply nourishes very dry skin, but is among the more pore-clogging plant oils.

Petroleum jelly — the gold-standard occlusive that seals in moisture and speeds healing of compromised skin barriers. Non-comedogenic despite the heavy, greasy feel, though it can feel too rich for oily skin.
An extract from a medicinal mushroom used in Korean skincare for antioxidant and skin-calming benefits.
A multifunctional preservative and solvent with a rose-like scent, also used to support fragrance. Generally well tolerated.
A widely used, broad-spectrum preservative that keeps products free of bacteria and mold. Used at low levels (under 1%) and generally well tolerated.
A silicone with added shine and a slightly different feel than dimethicone, used to add gloss and a smooth, non-tacky finish to skin and hair products.
A water-soluble chemical UVB filter that gives a light, non-greasy feel, often used in facial sunscreens and as a stabilizer for other filters.
A synthetic resorcinol derivative used as a potent tyrosinase inhibitor for brightening and dark spot correction; generally effective at low concentrations with a favorable tolerance profile.
pregnancy_notes: Limited dedicated pregnancy safety data; caution is reasonable in the absence of robust studies.

what_it_does: An antioxidant flavonoid derived from apples, often paired with vitamin C and ferulic acid to enhance antioxidant stability and skin penetration; generally well tolerated.
A mild plant-derived acid (from rice/grains) with gentle exfoliating, antioxidant, and chelating properties; sometimes included in brightening blends.
A plant-derived emollient and emulsifying ester blend that mimics natural skin lipids for a soft, conditioning finish.

A gentler antifungal alternative to zinc pyrithione, used in anti-dandruff and scalp-care formulas to control yeast overgrowth. Generally well tolerated with a lower irritation profile than zinc pyrithione.
A water-soluble, photostable UVB filter used mainly in European sunscreen formulations to add broad-spectrum coverage with low skin penetration.
A synthetic thickener and emulsion stabilizer that creates light, elegant gel textures while helping keep oil droplets evenly suspended.

A polymeric preservative (related to the antiseptic PHMB) used as a paraben-free alternative effective against bacteria, popular in 'sensitive skin' lines. Generally well tolerated, though regulatory bodies have flagged some safety questions at higher concentrations in certain product types.
pregnancy_notes: Limited long-term human data; commonly used in sensitive-skin products without major reported issues, but data gaps mean a cautious rating is appropriate.

what_it_does: A thick, glossy synthetic emollient commonly used in lip glosses and balms for shine and cushion.
A biofermented humectant that forms a breathable film on skin, holding in moisture and giving a smoothing, plumping effect comparable to hyaluronic acid.
An emulsifying emollient ester often used in water-in-oil formulas to stabilize the mix while providing a rich, cushiony skin feel.
A naturally derived emulsifier from sugar and glycerin that stabilizes rich, creamy textures and is often used in 'clean beauty' moisturizers.
A gentle, plant-derived emollient and emulsifying ester used in modern 'clean' formulations as a mild alternative to traditional surfactant emulsifiers.
A specialized emulsifier that stabilizes water-in-oil formulas, often used in sunscreens and richer creams to keep water droplets evenly dispersed in an oil base.
Japanese knotweed root extract, a natural source of resveratrol, providing strong antioxidant benefits; generally gentle and well tolerated.
A polymer-like ingredient used as a mild emulsifier and skin-feel enhancer in some peptide and acid serums; not an exfoliating acid despite the name.
A silicone-based powder that absorbs oil and gives skin a soft-focus, blurred finish, commonly used in primers and mattifying formulas.
A cellulose-derived conditioning polymer that smooths hair and skin and helps soften the after-feel of cleansing products by reducing friction.
A conditioning polymer that deposits onto hair and skin to reduce static, improve combability, and provide a soft after-feel, commonly used in cleansers and shampoos.
A film-forming silicone polymer that adds gloss and a smooth, flexible finish, often used in lip products and skin-finishing primers.
A silicone-bound UVB filter that stays on the skin's surface, offering effective sun protection with minimal absorption into skin.
A widely used, gentle nonionic emulsifier and solubilizer that helps blend oils, fragrance, and actives into water-based formulas without significant irritation.
A nonionic emulsifier that helps stabilize oil-in-water creams and lotions, contributing to a smooth, even texture.
A nonionic emulsifier and solubilizer used to disperse oils and fragrance in water-based products, common in toners, serums, and lotions.
Purslane extract rich in omega-3 fatty acids and antioxidants, used for soothing and barrier-supporting benefits in sensitive-skin formulas.
A gentler, water-soluble salt derivative of azelaic acid used for brightening and calming redness with less irritation potential than the parent acid.
A mild pH-buffering salt used to adjust and stabilize a formula's acidity. Gentle and rarely a source of irritation.
An amino-acid-based gentle surfactant derived from coconut, valued for soft, low-irritation foam in premium and sensitive-skin cleansers.
A mild, amino-acid-based surfactant derived from coconut and glycine, known for gentle, low-irritation cleansing with a soft, creamy lather.

A strong alkaline pH adjuster used in tiny amounts to neutralize acids and adjust formula pH, and in much higher amounts as the saponifying agent in liquid soaps. Can be quite irritating or even caustic at higher concentrations.
A gentle, widely used preservative derived from sorbic acid that protects formulas against mold and yeast growth.
An emollient and mild solvent used to improve the spreadability and skin feel of creams and lotions, especially fragrance and active dispersion.
A solubilizer commonly paired with PEG-40 hydrogenated castor oil to help disperse oils and active ingredients evenly into clear, water-based formulas.
A plant-derived glycol used as a humectant and preservative booster, popular in 'clean' formulas as a propylene-glycol alternative. Very well tolerated and rarely irritating.
A resin-like substance made by bees, used for its antioxidant and soothing properties; can occasionally cause allergic reactions in those sensitive to bee products.
pregnancy_notes: Generally considered low risk topically, but those with bee product allergies should avoid it.

what_it_does: A common humectant and solvent that hydrates and carries actives into the skin. Occasionally irritating for very sensitive skin at high levels.
An ester used mainly as an emollient and texture enhancer, occasionally noted in preservative-support systems for its mild antimicrobial contribution. Generally low-irritation.
A modern, lightweight ester emollient that gives a dry, silicone-like skin feel without using actual silicones; popular in 'silicone-free' formulas.

A paraben preservative that protects products from bacteria, yeast, and mold. The EU caps its concentration and restricts it from nappy-area products for children under three due to stronger hormone-mimicking activity than shorter-chain parabens, so it's a slightly higher-concern paraben than methyl- or ethylparaben.
pregnancy_notes: EU restricts use on the nappy area of young children over endocrine-activity concerns; most experts consider normal adult cosmetic exposure low-risk, but some advise minimizing exposure during pregnancy out of caution.

what_it_does: Sweet almond oil — a classic emollient rich in fatty acids and vitamin E, used to soften and nourish skin. Avoid if you have a tree nut allergy.
Apricot kernel oil — a light, fast-absorbing emollient similar in profile to sweet almond oil, suitable for normal to dry skin.
Japanese apricot (ume) fruit extract used for its antioxidant and mild brightening properties along with gentle natural acids.
Peach fruit extract used for its mild antioxidant and skin-conditioning benefits, lending a soft, hydrating feel to formulas.
A naturally fermented polysaccharide used as a film-forming thickener that gives a tightening, smoothing sensation and light hold, popular in 'clean' anti-aging formulas.
A blend of natural fruit enzymes and alpha hydroxy acids from pumpkin used in exfoliating masks for mild surface renewal; not a single standardized INCI but common on ingredient lists.
Pomegranate extract rich in punicalagins and other polyphenols, valued for antioxidant and mild brightening support against environmental damage; generally well tolerated.
Pomegranate seed oil rich in punicic acid, a polyunsaturated fatty acid that supports barrier repair and provides light antioxidant protection.
A film-forming polymer used in hairsprays, styling products, and some skincare to create a flexible film that holds shape or smooths skin texture.
Apple fruit extract that naturally contains AHAs, used for mild exfoliating and antioxidant benefits in gentle toners and essences.
An alpha-keto acid used in professional chemical peels for its exfoliating and collagen-stimulating effects, generally stronger and more irritating than typical AHAs used at home.
pregnancy_notes: Professional-strength chemical peels using keto acids are generally deferred until after pregnancy due to limited safety data and the intensity of the treatment.

what_it_does: A formaldehyde-releasing preservative considered one of the stronger sensitizers in this class, used in some rinse-off and leave-on products. Dermatologists often flag it for people with known formaldehyde or fragrance allergies.
pregnancy_notes: Among the more allergenic formaldehyde-releasers; reasonable to avoid for sensitive skin and during pregnancy as a precaution.

what_it_does: A keratolytic and antiseptic compound used in some acne and anti-blemish treatments to help slough dead skin and reduce bacteria; can be irritating and is restricted in concentration by regulation.
pregnancy_notes: Data on topical cosmetic use in pregnancy is limited; used cautiously and typically avoided in hair dye and acne formulas during pregnancy per general precaution.

what_it_does: A polyphenol antioxidant originally found in grapes and red wine that helps neutralize free radicals and supports skin's defense against environmental stress; generally well tolerated with limited but promising anti-aging evidence.
A vitamin A derivative one conversion step closer to retinoic acid than retinol, making it more potent and faster-acting than retinol but generally better tolerated than prescription tretinoin.
pregnancy_notes: As a vitamin A derivative in the retinoid family, it is advised against during pregnancy due to teratogenicity concerns associated with retinoids as a class.

what_it_does: A gold-standard vitamin A derivative that boosts cell turnover, smooths fine lines, and improves texture and tone. Can irritate; introduce slowly and always pair with SPF.
pregnancy_notes: Topical retinoids are generally avoided during pregnancy and nursing; consult your doctor.

what_it_does: A mild ester form of vitamin A that must convert through several steps to become active retinoic acid, making it gentler but slower-acting than retinol.
pregnancy_notes: As a vitamin A/retinoid derivative, avoided during pregnancy per class-wide precautionary guidance despite its mild potency.

what_it_does: A vitamin A ester combining retinol with linoleic acid, providing gentle antioxidant and skin-conditioning benefits with minimal irritation.
pregnancy_notes: As a retinoid/vitamin A derivative, avoided during pregnancy following class-wide precautionary guidance.

what_it_does: A gentler, more stable retinoid ester. Milder than retinol but also less potent. Converts to retinol then retinoic acid in the skin.
pregnancy_notes: Retinoids are typically avoided in pregnancy; consult your doctor.

what_it_does: A combined ester pairing retinyl palmitate with tartaric acid, marketed as a stabilized, gentler retinoid-acid hybrid for sensitive skin anti-aging formulas.
pregnancy_notes: Contains a vitamin A ester; advised against in pregnancy following standard retinoid precautions.

what_it_does: A gentle vitamin A ester that converts slowly to retinoic acid, offering mild anti-aging benefits with lower irritation potential than retinol.
pregnancy_notes: As a retinoid, avoided during pregnancy following class-wide precautionary guidance despite being a mild derivative.

what_it_does: A hybrid retinoid ester combining retinol and retinoic acid properties, designed to offer effective anti-aging action with reduced irritation compared to free retinoic acid.
pregnancy_notes: As a retinoid, this is advised against during pregnancy following class-wide precautionary guidance.

what_it_does: Castor oil — a thick, humectant-like oil traditionally used on lashes, brows, and as a thickening agent in lip and balm products. Can be drying to skin in pure form despite popular claims.
pregnancy_notes: High oral doses are linked to inducing labor; topical cosmetic use is generally considered low-risk, but check with your doctor.

what_it_does: Rosehip seed oil, rich in linoleic acid and natural vitamin A precursors, that helps nourish and support barrier repair and fade the look of scars and discoloration.
pregnancy_notes: Contains trace natural retinoid-like compounds; many choose to avoid concentrated rosehip oil in pregnancy as a precaution.

what_it_does: Cabbage rose flower extract used for mild antioxidant and soothing benefits along with a natural floral scent.
Rose flower water (rosewater) used as a fragrant, lightly soothing base liquid in toners and mists; the fragrance compounds can be sensitizing for some.

Rosemary leaf extract containing carnosic acid and rosmarinic acid, used as a natural antioxidant that also helps stabilize oils against rancidity; can be mildly irritating for sensitive skin in concentrated form.
A nutrient-rich bee product extract used in anti-aging formulas for its amino acids and reputed skin-conditioning benefits.
pregnancy_notes: Those with bee product allergies should avoid it; otherwise considered low risk topically.

what_it_does: A humectant derived from plant sugars that closely mimics the skin's own moisture-binding sugars, offering 24-hour-plus hydration with a non-sticky feel.

A yeast-derived ferment filtrate (popularized by SK-II's 'Pitera') used for antioxidant, hydrating, and brightening benefits; generally gentle, though ferment filtrates can occasionally be sensitizing for reactive skin.
Sugar cane extract that is a natural source of glycolic acid, providing mild exfoliation in 'natural' AHA formulations.
pregnancy_notes: Contains natural glycolic acid; treated the same as glycolic acid in pregnancy guidance, generally fine at low levels.

what_it_does: A BHA that exfoliates inside the pore, dissolving oil and dead skin. Excellent for blackheads, acne, and oily skin. Oil-soluble so it gets deep.
pregnancy_notes: Low-concentration topical use is generally considered okay, but check with your doctor.

what_it_does: White willow bark extract that contains salicin, a natural precursor to salicylic acid, giving it mild exfoliating and anti-acne benefits.
pregnancy_notes: Contains salicin, related to salicylic acid; use with the same caution as BHA during pregnancy.

what_it_does: Elderflower extract with mild astringent and soothing properties, traditionally used to calm and brighten tired-looking skin.
Sandalwood essential oil used for fragrance and a traditional calming reputation, though essential oils in general carry a real fragrance-allergy risk.
An antioxidant-rich berry extract used in anti-aging and protective formulas to help combat environmental free-radical stress; generally gentle.
Marula oil — a lightweight, antioxidant-rich oil that absorbs quickly and softens skin without leaving a heavy residue.
A naturally fermented polysaccharide thickener that creates a unique, cushiony gel texture, often marketed as a 'clean' alternative to carbomer.
Baikal skullcap root extract containing baicalin, valued for strong antioxidant and anti-inflammatory/soothing properties; commonly used in barrier-supportive and calming formulas.
Sesame seed extract used for its antioxidant lignans and mild skin-conditioning properties, often paired with sesame oil in nourishing formulas.
Sesame seed oil — a moderate-weight emollient with mild antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds, used in body oils and traditional formulas.
A biotech-derived form of epidermal growth factor (EGF) used in serums marketed to accelerate skin repair and renewal; potency and stability in finished products varies widely.
pregnancy_notes: Growth factor ingredients lack dedicated pregnancy safety studies; many formulators recommend avoiding them during pregnancy out of caution given their biological signaling activity.

what_it_does: A recombinant human growth factor (often a form of EGF or related protein) used in regenerative serums to support cell turnover and wound-healing-like skin renewal.
pregnancy_notes: No dedicated pregnancy safety data; growth-factor ingredients are generally approached with caution during pregnancy.

what_it_does: A biotech-derived growth factor protein (often related to FGF, fibroblast growth factor) used in anti-aging serums to support collagen synthesis signaling.
pregnancy_notes: No dedicated pregnancy safety data; growth-factor ingredients are generally approached with caution during pregnancy.

what_it_does: A fine mineral powder used to absorb oil, mattify the skin's surface, and give a soft-focus blurring effect; also used to thicken and stabilize formulas.
A silicone-treated silica powder that absorbs oil and gives a smooth, blurred, mattifying finish, common in primers and powder sunscreens.

An inorganic antimicrobial agent used as an alternative preservative technology in some modern formulas, leveraging silver's natural germ-fighting properties. Considered low-irritation at the very low levels used.
pregnancy_notes: Newer preservative technology with less long-term safety data than traditional preservatives; reasonable to apply a cautious rating pending more research.

what_it_does: Another silver-based antimicrobial preservative technology, used in small amounts to inhibit bacterial growth as an alternative to conventional preservatives. Generally well tolerated.
pregnancy_notes: Limited long-term safety data compared to established preservatives; a cautious rating reflects that data gap rather than known harm.

what_it_does: Milk thistle extract containing silymarin, a flavonoid antioxidant studied for skin-protective and anti-inflammatory effects; gentle and often used in soothing or anti-pollution formulas.
A blend of dimethicone and silica gas-reducing agent mainly known as an oral anti-flatulent, but also used topically as an occlusive, protective skin-smoothing agent.
Jojoba oil — technically a liquid wax that closely mimics skin's natural sebum. Excellent emollient that absorbs well and is non-comedogenic for most users.
A filtrate of snail mucin containing glycoproteins, hyaluronic acid, and allantoin-like compounds that hydrate, soothe, and support skin repair.
A water-soluble thickening polymer used to build viscosity and create gel-like, jelly textures in moisturizers and serums.
A plant-derived preservative-boosting salt, often used together with sodium levulinate in natural preservative blends. Generally gentle and low-irritation.
A buffered, less acidic salt form of vitamin C used for antioxidant benefits with reduced irritation potential compared to pure ascorbic acid, though it's typically less potent for brightening.

A stable, water-soluble vitamin C derivative that converts to ascorbic acid in the skin, offering antioxidant and mild brightening benefits with much better stability than pure vitamin C and good gentleness even for sensitive or acne-prone skin.
A widely used preservative that protects formulas from microbial growth, often paired with other preservatives or acids for broader protection.
Common baking soda, used as a mild abrasive/cleansing agent and pH adjuster in some scrubs and oral care products. Can disrupt skin's natural pH if overused.
An effective, sulfate-family cleansing agent that produces a rich lather, commonly used in body washes and bar cleansers as a budget-friendly foaming surfactant.
A naturally derived, mild surfactant and conditioning agent often used in sulfate-free shampoos and cleansers for gentle, low-irritation foam.
An alkaline pH adjuster, sometimes used in cleansers and bath products to raise pH. Generally mild at low use levels but can be drying or irritating in larger amounts.
A cellulose gum derivative used to thicken water-based formulas and stabilize emulsions, giving a smooth, gel-like consistency.
Common table salt, used as a thickener in surfactant-based cleansers and as an exfoliating ingredient in some scrubs.
A gentle pH adjuster and buffering agent derived from citric acid, used to fine-tune a formula's acidity for stability and skin comfort. Very well tolerated.
A gentler relative of sodium laureth sulfate derived from coconut, used as a foaming, oil-cutting cleansing agent in body washes and shampoos.
A mild, coconut-derived amphoteric surfactant used to cleanse gently and boost foam, often included in baby and sensitive-skin cleansers.
A mild amphoteric surfactant used to reduce irritation and stabilize foam in gentle face and body cleansers.
A gentle amino-acid-derived surfactant from coconut and alanine, used for its mild, low-irritation cleansing in premium sensitive-skin face washes.
A gentle, amino-acid-derived surfactant made from coconut and glutamic acid, prized for its soft, low-irritation lather and use in premium Japanese-style cleansers.
A very mild coconut-derived cleansing agent famous for the silky, non-drying lather it gives in syndet (soap-free) bars like Dove, well tolerated by dry and sensitive skin.
A coconut-derived, amino-acid-based gentle surfactant that cleanses with a soft lather and is well tolerated by sensitive and acne-prone skin.
The water-soluble sodium salt of dehydroacetic acid, used as a mild preservative against fungi and some bacteria. Frequently paired with benzyl alcohol in 'paraben-free' preservative systems.
A mild, sugar-derived chelating agent used as a gentler, more 'natural' alternative to EDTA in formulas, helping to stabilize the product by binding metal ions. Very well tolerated.
The salt form of hyaluronic acid. A powerful humectant that holds many times its weight in water for plumper, more hydrated skin, with better penetration than HA itself.
A cross-linked, film-forming form of hyaluronic acid used for longer-lasting surface hydration and a smoothing effect, distinct from the standard humectant Sodium Hyaluronate.
A strong alkali used in tiny amounts to adjust and balance a product's pH. Fully neutralized in the finished formula.

A formaldehyde-releasing preservative often marketed as a gentler alternative within this class, used in rinse-off and some leave-on products. Still carries some risk of triggering reactions in formaldehyde-sensitive individuals.
pregnancy_notes: Lower-release formaldehyde donor, but caution is still reasonable for very sensitive skin or during pregnancy.

what_it_does: The salt form of lactic acid, used primarily as a humectant and mild pH adjuster rather than for active exfoliation, gentler than free lactic acid.
A common foaming cleanser/surfactant that's milder than SLS. Effective at removing oil and dirt but can be drying for very sensitive skin in high amounts.
A gentle amphoteric surfactant that cleanses with reduced irritation potential, frequently used in formulas marketed for sensitive skin and babies.
An amino-acid-derived gentle surfactant that cleanses with a soft, conditioning lather and is well tolerated by dry and sensitive skin.
A gentle, coconut-derived cleansing agent prized for its soft, low-irritation lather, commonly found in premium cleansing bars and creamy face washes.
A mild, amino-acid-derived cleansing agent that creates a creamy lather while being gentler than sulfates, commonly found in face washes and toothpaste.
A strong foaming surfactant and known skin irritant at higher concentrations. Cleanses well but can strip and dry the skin barrier.
A mild, low-irritation cleansing agent often used in foaming bath products and bath bombs for its gentle lather and good solubility.
A naturally derived preservative booster used alongside other mild preservatives in 'natural' formulas, helping to control microbial growth. Well tolerated by most skin types.

An alkaline pH-adjusting and cleansing-boosting ingredient, mainly used in wash-off products like cleansers to help adjust acidity and improve cleaning performance. Can be irritating to skin and eyes at higher concentrations.
A mild, coconut-derived taurate surfactant that cleanses gently while producing a creamy lather, used in moisturizing cleansing bars and washes.

The water-soluble sodium salt form of methylparaben, used as a preservative in formulas where better solubility at a given pH is needed. Shares methylparaben's well-established, low-irritation safety profile.
An ethoxylated sulfate surfactant similar to sodium laureth sulfate, used for its strong foaming and cleansing properties in washes and shampoos.
A natural component of skin's own moisturizing factor (NMF). A strong humectant that binds water to the skin without feeling heavy or sticky.
A buffering and pH-adjusting salt used to keep a formula's acidity stable over its shelf life. Low irritation potential.
Another name sometimes used for disodium phosphate; a pH-buffering salt that helps keep a formula's acidity consistent over time. Generally inert and well tolerated.

A plant-derived chelating agent (from phytic acid, found in grains and legumes) used as a more 'natural-origin' alternative to EDTA to bind metal ions and improve product stability. Gentle and well tolerated.
A synthetic polymer thickener that gels water-based formulas, also valued for its ability to create a soft, velvety, blurring finish on skin.
A starch-based absorbent thickener used to create lightweight, oil-absorbing gel and powder textures, sometimes used in mattifying formulas.
The salt form of polyglutamic acid, a film-forming humectant that helps the skin hold onto water and improves the after-feel of hydrating products.
The sodium salt form of propylparaben, used for better water solubility. Carries the same EU use restrictions and mild endocrine-activity caveats as propylparaben itself.
pregnancy_notes: Same endocrine-activity considerations as propylparaben.

what_it_does: A prescription-grade antibacterial often combined with sulfur for rosacea and acne treatment, reducing inflammatory bacteria on the skin's surface.
pregnancy_notes: Limited pregnancy-specific data; topical sulfa antibiotics are generally used only under medical supervision during pregnancy.

what_it_does: An ethoxylated sulfate surfactant that provides moderate foam and cleansing power, somewhat milder than sodium lauryl sulfate.
A traditional herb root extract used in Korean and Chinese skincare for its calming and clarifying properties on blemish-prone skin.

A naturally derived organic acid preservative (originally found in rowan berries) that fights mold and yeast, usually paired with other preservatives since it doesn't cover bacteria as well on its own. Can occasionally cause mild irritation in sensitive skin.
A naturally derived emulsifier from olive oil and sorbitol that helps blend oil and water phases while adding a touch of light conditioning.
A common co-emulsifier derived from sorbitol that helps stabilize creams and lotions, frequently paired with sucrose stearate or other emulsifiers.
A sugar-alcohol humectant that draws water into the skin and helps keep formulas from drying out. Common in moisturizers and toothpaste alike.
Blue-green algae extract rich in antioxidants and amino acids, used to help protect skin from oxidative stress and support a healthy-looking complexion; generally gentle.
A stable, lightweight emollient (often plant-derived) that mimics skin's own oils. Softens and reduces water loss without a greasy feel. Great for most skin types.
The unsaturated precursor to squalane, naturally found in human sebum and plant sources. Less stable than its hydrogenated form (squalane) and now mostly replaced by it in modern formulas.
A cationic conditioning and emulsifying agent mainly used in hair conditioners; in skincare it can act as an antistatic and emulsion stabilizer.
A nonionic co-emulsifier often paired with steareth-21 to fine-tune the texture and stability of lotions and creams.
A nonionic PEG-based emulsifier that helps stabilize creams and lotions, contributing to a smooth, even, lightweight texture.
A PEG-based nonionic emulsifier used to help stabilize lightweight oil-in-water lotions and create a smooth, even texture.
A naturally occurring fatty acid used as an emulsifier and thickener that gives lotions and creams body and a rich, stable texture.
A waxy silicone derivative that helps stabilize water-in-silicone emulsions and adds a smooth, water-resistant feel, often found in sunscreens and long-wear makeup.
An organic acid used as a pH adjuster and, in some formulas, for mild anti-acne or sebum-balancing properties. Generally mild with low irritation potential.
A mild, sugar-derived emulsifier that helps stabilize lotions and creams while contributing a soft, smooth skin feel.
A traditional keratolytic and antimicrobial acne treatment that helps dry out blemishes and reduce oil; has a distinctive smell and can be drying.
An enzyme antioxidant that neutralizes superoxide free radicals, sometimes included in high-end anti-aging formulas; gentle but with limited robust clinical efficacy data for topical use.
Comfrey leaf extract containing allantoin naturally, used for its soothing and skin-conditioning properties, though comfrey root itself raises safety concerns and is restricted in some markets.
pregnancy_notes: Comfrey contains trace pyrrolizidine alkaloids of concern in some forms; leaf-derived cosmetic extracts at regulated levels are generally considered low risk, but caution is advised.

what_it_does: A lab-grown alternative to natural mica, used to add a smooth shimmer and light-reflecting finish to makeup and tinted skincare without the mining-related sourcing concerns of natural mica.
A mineral powder used to absorb oil and provide a silky slip in powders, eyeshadows, and some lotions. Cosmetic-grade talc is required to be asbestos-free.
An AHA found in grapes that exfoliates and also functions as a pH adjuster/chelator in many formulas, usually used in combination with other acids.
pregnancy_notes: Treated like other AHAs in pregnancy; no specific contraindication is established but moderation is advised.

what_it_does: A potent prescription retinoid used for psoriasis and acne that converts to its active form in skin; one of the stronger and more irritating topical retinoids.
pregnancy_notes: Carries a pregnancy contraindication on its prescribing label due to teratogenicity demonstrated in animal studies.

what_it_does: A triethanolamine salt of lauryl sulfate that cleanses and foams well but can be more irritating to skin and eyes than gentler surfactant alternatives.
A water-soluble UVA filter developed specifically for strong, stable UVA protection; a long-standing staple of European sun care.

A fat-soluble, highly stable vitamin C ester that blends easily into oils and emulsions; well tolerated, with good antioxidant and brightening performance and lower irritation than pure ascorbic acid.

A chelating agent that binds trace metal ions in a formula, preventing them from degrading the product or interfering with preservatives. Considered safe and non-irritating at the low levels used in cosmetics, though there's ongoing environmental discussion about its biodegradability.
Cacao extract used for its antioxidant polyphenols, sometimes included in anti-aging and skin-conditioning formulas for its protective benefits.
Cocoa butter — a thick, richly emollient butter prized for its scent and texture, but quite occlusive and known to be comedogenic for breakout-prone skin.

Also called alpha lipoic acid, a potent antioxidant that works in both water and oil environments and helps regenerate other antioxidants like vitamin C and E; can be irritating at higher concentrations for sensitive skin.
pregnancy_notes: Limited safety data on topical use during pregnancy; many practitioners suggest caution due to insufficient research, though risk is considered low at cosmetic concentrations.

what_it_does: Thyme extract with natural antimicrobial compounds, sometimes used in blemish-control formulas, though it can be irritating at higher concentrations.
A mineral UV filter that reflects UV light, strong against UVB. Often paired with zinc oxide. Gentle and suitable for sensitive and reactive skin.
Vitamin E — a fat-soluble antioxidant that protects the skin barrier and works synergistically with vitamin C. Common and well tolerated.
A stable vitamin E ester that acts as an antioxidant and skin-conditioning agent, protecting the barrier. Common and gentle.
An ester of vitamin E and linoleic acid that provides antioxidant and emollient benefits, often used to help condition and protect the skin barrier; generally gentle.
A form of vitamin E with strong antioxidant activity, often used alongside tocopherol for enhanced free-radical protection and skin-conditioning benefits; very gentle.
A brightening active originally developed as a clotting medication, used topically to interrupt the pigment-production pathway and fade melasma and post-inflammatory dark spots.
pregnancy_notes: Oral tranexamic acid is contraindicated in pregnancy due to clotting-related risk; topical cosmetic use has limited dedicated safety data, so caution and checking with a provider is generally advised.

what_it_does: A sugar-based humectant that helps cells retain water and resist stress, used in hydrating and barrier-supportive formulas.

A prescription-strength retinoic acid that directly binds retinoid receptors without needing conversion, making it the most effective and most irritating topical retinoid for acne and anti-aging; available only by prescription in most countries.
pregnancy_notes: A known teratogen in oral form and contraindicated in pregnancy by prescribing guidelines; topical use is also generally discontinued before and during pregnancy as a precaution.

what_it_does: A strong caustic acid used in professional in-office chemical peels to cause controlled skin shedding; not used in leave-on retail cosmetics due to its high strength and burn risk.
pregnancy_notes: In-office TCA peels are generally deferred until after pregnancy due to the strength of the treatment and lack of safety data on systemic absorption during controlled peeling.

what_it_does: An antibacterial agent chemically related to triclosan, once used in bar soaps and washes. Banned from over-the-counter antiseptic washes in the US and facing similar endocrine-disruption concerns as triclosan.
pregnancy_notes: Shares triclosan's endocrine-disruption concerns; best avoided during pregnancy and in general.

what_it_does: An antibacterial agent once common in soaps and deodorants, now heavily restricted (and banned outright in some regions and product categories) over concerns about hormone disruption and antibiotic resistance.
pregnancy_notes: Classified as a possible endocrine disruptor with absorption through skin; banned from many product types in the EU and US, and best avoided during pregnancy.

what_it_does: A nonionic surfactant used mainly as a solubilizer to help disperse oils and fragrance evenly into water-based formulas like toners and essences.

A common pH adjuster that neutralizes acidic ingredients (like carbomer gels) and helps stabilize formulas. Generally safe at the low residual levels used in finished products, though it can be irritating in higher concentrations and some studies have raised contamination concerns from nitrosamine formation with certain nitrosating agents.
A lightweight, glycerin-based ester emollient with a dry, silky finish that helps disperse pigments and sunscreen filters evenly.
A newer fourth-generation prescription retinoid selective for RAR-gamma receptors, used for acne with a more targeted action and a more tolerable side-effect profile.
pregnancy_notes: As a retinoid, it carries the same class-wide teratogenicity precaution and is avoided during pregnancy.

what_it_does: A silicone resin that adds shine and a smooth, glassy finish to skin and hair, often used to boost gloss in serums and hair-finishing products.
A film-forming silicone resin that adds a long-wearing, semi-matte finish and water resistance to makeup and skincare formulas.
A copper-free version of the GHK tripeptide used to support the skin's natural repair signaling and collagen production without the coloration of the copper-bound form.
pregnancy_notes: Limited dedicated pregnancy safety data; cautious approach generally recommended for novel peptides.

what_it_does: Another sodium salt form of EDTA used as a chelating agent to bind metal ions and help preservatives and antioxidants work more effectively. Functionally similar to disodium and tetrasodium EDTA with the same excellent safety profile.
Wheat germ oil — a heavy, vitamin E-rich oil that nourishes very dry skin but is fairly comedogenic and should be avoided by those with wheat allergies or breakout-prone skin.
A salicylate-based chemical UVB filter occasionally used in older sunscreen and after-sun formulations for mild sun protection.
pregnancy_notes: Limited modern safety data; salicylate filters are typically used at low levels, but oral/high-dose salicylate exposure is a pregnancy concern, so topical use should be modest.

what_it_does: A lesser-known AHA used occasionally in mild chemical exfoliating blends, functioning similarly to other fruit acids in promoting surface cell turnover.
pregnancy_notes: Limited specific data; treated like other AHAs with general caution advised during pregnancy.

what_it_does: The reduced, more bioavailable form of CoQ10; functions as an antioxidant that helps combat free-radical damage and is used in some premium anti-aging formulas, though human topical efficacy data is limited.

Also known as CoQ10, a naturally occurring antioxidant that helps protect skin cells from oxidative stress and supports cellular energy production; gentle and suitable for most skin types, though clinical evidence for visible anti-aging effects is modest.
A mild, fatty-acid-derived ingredient with anti-microbial and sebum-regulating properties, often used in natural-leaning preservative systems and acne-focused formulas. Generally gentle.
A biomimetic peptide marketed to calm overactive sebaceous activity and reduce breakouts by modulating melanocyte-stimulating-hormone receptor signaling associated with oil production.
pregnancy_notes: No dedicated pregnancy safety data; general caution applied to novel peptide actives.

what_it_does: A naturally occurring component of skin's own moisturizing factor that hydrates and gently helps soften and exfoliate rough, thickened skin at higher concentrations.
Stinging nettle extract used for its mild anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties in calming and scalp-care formulas.
A silicone elastomer powder used to absorb sebum and blur the look of pores and texture, giving a soft, suede-like matte finish.
Grape fruit extract used for mild antioxidant and skin-conditioning benefits, often paired with grape seed extract in anti-aging formulas.
Grape seed extract rich in proanthocyanidins, used for its strong antioxidant activity to help fight environmental free-radical damage; gentle and well tolerated.
Grapeseed oil — a lightweight, linoleic-acid-rich oil that absorbs quickly and suits oily or acne-prone skin better than heavier oils.
The most common cosmetic ingredient. Acts as a solvent that dissolves other ingredients and forms the base of most water-based formulas.
A natural thickener and stabilizer that gives products a gel-like texture. Inert on skin and broadly compatible.
A mineral (physical) UV filter that reflects and scatters UVA and UVB. Broad-spectrum, photostable, and gentle — a top choice for sensitive skin sunscreens.
A zinc salt that helps regulate sebum production and has mild antibacterial properties, commonly used in formulas targeting oily and acne-prone skin.

A potent antifungal and antibacterial agent best known as the active ingredient in anti-dandruff shampoos, also used as a preservative-adjacent active in some scalp and acne formulas. Can be irritating with prolonged or frequent leave-on contact.
pregnancy_notes: Limited absorption data for skin/scalp use; many providers suggest minimizing use of leave-on, high-concentration formulas during pregnancy out of caution.

what_it_does: A fatty acid zinc salt used in powders and cosmetics to improve adhesion, water resistance, and smooth slip on skin.
